#### 2. **Load Rwanda shapefile and Gishwati-Mukura reserve shapefile**

If you don’t already have shapefiles:Get **Rwanda** boundaries from
`rnaturalearth`.

-   Use shapefiles for **Gishwati-Mukura** Biosphere (download from a
    reliable GIS source or governmental portal).

```{r}


# Set Rwanda-specific projection (UTM Zone 35S)
rwanda_crs <- 32735  # EPSG:32735 for Rwanda (WGS 84 / UTM zone 35S)

# Get Rwanda map and reproject
rwanda <- ne_countries(scale = "medium", country = "Rwanda", returnclass = "sf") %>%
  st_transform(crs = rwanda_crs)

# Set directory path
shapefile_dir <- "/Users/blaise/Rwanda_National Parks/Code_Species_Modeling/Gishwati_Mukura"

# Read and reproject Gishwati and Mukura shapefiles
gishwati <- st_read(file.path(shapefile_dir, "Gishwati_core.shp")) %>%
  st_transform(crs = rwanda_crs)
mukura <- st_read(file.path(shapefile_dir, "Mukura_core.shp")) %>%
  st_transform(crs = rwanda_crs)
gishwati_buffer <- st_read(file.path(shapefile_dir, "Gishwati_buffer.shp")) %>%
  st_transform(crs = rwanda_crs)
mukura_buffer <- st_read(file.path(shapefile_dir, "Mukura_buffer.shp")) %>%
  st_transform(crs = rwanda_crs)


```

```{r}
library(dplyr)
gishwati <- gishwati %>% mutate(source = "Gishwati_core") %>% select(source, geometry)
mukura <- mukura %>% mutate(source = "Mukura_core") %>% select(source, geometry)
gishwati_buffer <- gishwati_buffer %>% mutate(source = "Gishwati_buffer") %>% select(source, geometry)
mukura_buffer <- mukura_buffer %>% mutate(source = "Mukura_buffer") %>% select(source, geometry)

# Combine all with a source column
gmb_reserve <- rbind(gishwati, mukura, gishwati_buffer, mukura_buffer)

```

```{r}
unique(st_geometry_type(gmb_reserve))




```

```{r}
plot(gmb_reserve)
```


```{r}
# Set the directory path
shapefile_dir <- "/Users/blaise/Rwanda_National Parks/Code_Species_Modeling/Gishwati_Mukura"
```

```{r}
# Set the directory path
shapefile_dir <- "/Users/blaise/Rwanda_National Parks/Code_Species_Modeling/Gishwati_Mukura"

# List all .shp files in the directory (recursively, if needed)
shapefiles <- list.files(path = shapefile_dir, pattern = "\\.shp$", full.names = TRUE, recursive = TRUE)

# Print the list
print(shapefiles)

```

```{r}
library(sf)
library(dplyr)

# 1. Set Rwanda-specific projection (WGS 84 / UTM zone 35S)
rwanda_crs <- 32735  # EPSG code

# 2. Set shapefile directory path
shapefile_dir <- "/Users/blaise/Rwanda_National Parks/Code_Species_Modeling/Gishwati_Mukura/Rwanda-shapfile/Data_GishwatiMukura"

# 3. Read all necessary shapefiles
rwanda_boundary <- st_read(file.path(shapefile_dir, "National boundary_Rwanda.shp"))
lakes           <- st_read(file.path(shapefile_dir, "Lake.shp"))
lake_kivu       <- st_read(file.path(shapefile_dir, "Lake Kivu.shp"))
volcanoes       <- st_read(file.path(shapefile_dir, "Volcanoes N.P.shp"))
akagera         <- st_read(file.path(shapefile_dir, "Akagera NP.shp"))
Protected_area  <- st_read(file.path(shapefile_dir, "Nyungwe_Gishwati_Mukura_Rugezii_Burera_Ruhondo.shp"))
District<-st_read(file.path(shapefile_dir,"Rwanda_District.shp"))
# 4. Reproject all to Rwanda UTM CRS (EPSG:32735)
rwanda_boundary <- st_transform(rwanda_boundary, crs = rwanda_crs)
lakes           <- st_transform(lakes,           crs = rwanda_crs)
lake_kivu       <- st_transform(lake_kivu,       crs = rwanda_crs)
volcanoes       <- st_transform(volcanoes,       crs = rwanda_crs)
akagera         <- st_transform(akagera,         crs = rwanda_crs)
Protected_area  <- st_transform(Protected_area,  crs = rwanda_crs)
District  <- st_transform(District,  crs = rwanda_crs)
# 5. Store all as named layers for easy access
rwanda_layers <- list(
  National_boundary   = rwanda_boundary,
  Lakes      = lakes,
  Lake_kivu  = lake_kivu,
  Volcanoes  = volcanoes,
  Akagera    = akagera,
  Protected_area = Protected_area,
  Districts= District
)

# 6. If you want a simple variable called `rwanda` for mapping:
#rwanda <- rwanda_boundary

```

```{r}
library(ggplot2)

ggplot() +
  geom_sf(data = rwanda_layers$National_boundary, fill = "white", color = "black") +
  geom_sf(data = rwanda_layers$Districts, fill = "white", color = "black")+
  geom_sf(data = rwanda_layers$Lakes, fill = "lightblue", color = NA) +
  geom_sf(data = rwanda_layers$Lake_kivu, fill = "lightblue", color = NA) +
  geom_sf(data = rwanda_layers$Akagera, fill = "darkgreen", alpha = 0.3) +
  geom_sf(data = rwanda_layers$Volcanoes, fill = "darkgreen", size = 1) +
  geom_sf(data = rwanda_layers$Protected_area, fill = "darkgreen", size = 1) +
  coord_sf(crs = rwanda_crs) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Rwanda Map with Key Features")

```

```{r}
# --- Load required libraries ---
library(ggplot2)
library(ggspatial)   # provides annotation_north_arrow() and annotation_scale()
library(sf)

# --- Example CRS (replace with your actual CRS object if needed) ---
# rwanda_crs <- st_crs(4326)

# --- Create Map ---
ggplot() +
  geom_sf(data = rwanda_layers$National_boundary, fill = "white", color = "black") +
  geom_sf(data = rwanda_layers$Districts, fill = NA, color = "grey50", size = 0.3) +
  geom_sf(data = rwanda_layers$Lakes, fill = "lightblue", color = NA) +
  geom_sf(data = rwanda_layers$Lake_kivu, fill = "lightblue", color = NA) +
  geom_sf(data = rwanda_layers$Akagera, fill = "darkgreen", alpha = 0.3) +
  geom_sf(data = rwanda_layers$Volcanoes, fill = "darkgreen", size = 1) +
  geom_sf(data = rwanda_layers$Protected_area, fill = "darkgreen", size = 1) +
  coord_sf(crs = rwanda_crs) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Rwanda Map with Key Features",
    x = "Longitude",
    y = "Latitude"
  ) +
  # --- Add scale bar ---
  annotation_scale(
    location = "br",          # bottom left
    width_hint = 0.3,         # relative width
    text_cex = 0.8,
    line_width = 0.7
  ) +
  # --- Add north arrow (fancy or simple styles available) ---
  annotation_north_arrow(
    location = "tl",          # top left
    which_north = "true",
    style = north_arrow_fancy_orienteering,
    height = unit(1.5, "cm"),
    width = unit(1.5, "cm")
  )

```

```{r}
outdir <- "/Users/blaise/Rwanda_National Parks/Code_Species_Modeling/Ensemble_Mukura_Figures"
if (!dir.exists(outdir)) dir.create(outdir, recursive = TRUE)

ggsave(
  filename = file.path(outdir, "Rwanda_Map_Key_Features.pdf"),
  width = 8, height = 6,
  dpi = 600,
  useDingbats = FALSE
)

```

Updates map\

```{r}
convert_id_to_char <- function(x) {
  if ("id" %in% colnames(x)) {
    x$id <- as.character(x$id)
  }
  return(x)
}

# Apply to each layer
national_boundary <- convert_id_to_char(national_boundary)
lakes <- convert_id_to_char(lakes)
lake_kivu <- convert_id_to_char(lake_kivu)
akagera <- convert_id_to_char(akagera)
volcanoes <- convert_id_to_char(volcanoes)
protected_area <- convert_id_to_char(protected_area)

# Then bind
rwanda_combined <- bind_rows(
  national_boundary,
  lakes,
  lake_kivu,
  akagera,
  volcanoes,
  protected_area
)

```

```{r}
rwanda_map<-ggplot() +
  geom_sf(data = rwanda_combined %>% filter(layer_type == "Protected Area"),
          aes(fill = layer_type), color = NA) +
  geom_sf(data = rwanda_combined %>% filter(layer_type == "Lakes"),
          aes(fill = layer_type), color = NA) +
  geom_sf(data = rwanda_combined %>% filter(layer_type == "National Boundary"),
          aes(color = layer_type), fill = NA, size = 1) +
  coord_sf(crs = rwanda_crs) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    name = "Features",
    values = c(
      "Lakes" = "lightblue",
      "Protected Area" = "darkgreen"
    )
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(
    name = "Features",
    values = c("National Boundary" = "black")
  ) +
  guides(
    fill = guide_legend(order = 1),
    color = guide_legend(order = 2)
  ) +
  annotation_north_arrow(
    location = "tl",
    which_north = "true",
    style = north_arrow_fancy_orienteering
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Rwanda Map with Key Features",
    fill = "Legend",
    color = "Legend"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
print(rwanda_map)
```

```{r}
ggsave(
  filename = "rwanda_map_highres.png",
  plot = rwanda_map,
  width = 10, height = 8, units = "in", dpi = 600
)


```

```{r}
install.packages("svglite")
library(svglite)
ggsave(
  filename = "rwanda_map_vector.svg",
  plot = rwanda_map,
  width = 10, height = 8, units = "in"
)

```

```{r}
# Read FIA plot data from CSV
FiaPlot_dir <- "/Users/blaise/Rwanda_National Parks/Code_Species_Modeling/code"
#fia_csv <- read.csv(file.path(FiaPlot_dir, "groundtruth_data.csv"))
fia_csv <- read.csv(file.path(FiaPlot_dir, "fia_flagged_coordinates_2.csv"))

```

Add a negative sign to those columns for only "Gishwati"

```{r}
library(dplyr)

fia_csv <- fia_csv %>%
  mutate(across(
    .cols = c(Corner_1.Latitude,  Corner_2.Latitude, Corner_3.Latitude, Corner_4.Latitude),
    .fns = ~ ifelse(Site == "Gishwati", -abs(.), .)
  ))

```

Replace wrong value

```{r}
fia_csv<- fia_csv %>%
  mutate(
    Corner_1.Latitude = ifelse(Polygon_ID == "P112", -1.96886, Corner_1.Latitude),
    Corner_4.Latitude = ifelse(Polygon_ID == "P104", -2.02194, Corner_4.Latitude),
     Corner_2.Longitude = ifelse(Polygon_ID == "GMT004", 29.38399, Corner_2.Longitude)
  )

```

```{r}
# Convert FIA plot data to sf object and reproject
fia_plots <- st_as_sf(fia_csv, coords = c("Central_coordinate_Longitude", "Central_coordinate_Latitude"), crs = 4326) %>%
  st_transform(crs = rwanda_crs)
```

```{r}
library(dplyr)

# Remove plots P059 and P095
fia_csv <- fia_csv %>%
  filter(!Polygon_ID %in% c("P059", "P095"))

```

# Recalculate central coordinates as the mean of four corners

```{r}

# Recalculate central coordinates as the mean of four corners
fia_csv  <- fia_csv %>%
  mutate(
    Central_coordinate_Latitude = rowMeans(select(., Corner_1.Latitude, Corner_2.Latitude, Corner_3.Latitude, Corner_4.Latitude), na.rm = TRUE),
    Central_coordinate_Longitude = rowMeans(select(., Corner_1.Longitude, Corner_2.Longitude, Corner_3.Longitude, Corner_4.Longitude), na.rm = TRUE)
  )

# Convert to sf using new central coordinates
fia_sf <- st_as_sf(fia_csv , coords = c("Central_coordinate_Longitude", "Central_coordinate_Latitude"), crs = 4326)

# Transform to Rwanda UTM CRS
fia_plots <- st_transform(fia_sf, crs = 32735)

```

```{r}

# Define typical Rwanda coordinate bounds
library(dplyr)

# Define Rwanda's latitude and longitude bounds
lat_bounds <- c(-2.9, -1.0)
lon_bounds <- c(28.8, 30.9)

# Add flag column
fia_flagged <- fia_csv  %>%
  mutate(
    coord_flag = ifelse(
      Corner_1.Latitude < lat_bounds[1] | Corner_1.Latitude > lat_bounds[2] |
      Corner_2.Latitude < lat_bounds[1] | Corner_2.Latitude > lat_bounds[2] |
      Corner_3.Latitude < lat_bounds[1] | Corner_3.Latitude > lat_bounds[2] |
      Corner_4.Latitude < lat_bounds[1] | Corner_4.Latitude > lat_bounds[2] |
      Central_coordinate_Latitude < lat_bounds[1] | Central_coordinate_Latitude > lat_bounds[2] |

      Corner_1.Longitude < lon_bounds[1] | Corner_1.Longitude > lon_bounds[2] |
      Corner_2.Longitude < lon_bounds[1] | Corner_2.Longitude > lon_bounds[2] |
      Corner_3.Longitude < lon_bounds[1] | Corner_3.Longitude > lon_bounds[2] |
      Corner_4.Longitude < lon_bounds[1] | Corner_4.Longitude > lon_bounds[2] |
      Central_coordinate_Longitude < lon_bounds[1] | Central_coordinate_Longitude > lon_bounds[2],
      "Suspicious", "OK"
    )
  )

# Save flagged dataset to CSV
write.csv(fia_flagged, "fia_flagged_coordinates2.csv", row.names = FALSE)

# Optionally print summary
table(fia_flagged$coord_flag)


```

```{r}
fig_gishwati <- ggplot() +
  geom_sf(data = gishwati_all, fill = "gray70", color = "black") +

           ylim = st_bbox(gishwati_all)[c("ymin", "ymax")],

print(fig_gishwati)
```

```{r}
# --- Libraries ---
library(ggplot2)
library(ggspatial)
library(dplyr)
library(sf)

# --- Output directory ---
outdir <- "/Users/blaise/Rwanda_National Parks/Code_Species_Modeling/Ensemble_Forest_Site_Maps"
if (!dir.exists(outdir)) dir.create(outdir, recursive = TRUE)

# --- Convert survey points to sf objects ---
sf_points_gishwati <- list(
  Acacia_mearnsii  = st_as_sf(acacia_mearnsii_points_gishwati,  coords = c("x", "y"), crs = 4326),
  Acacia_melanoxylon = st_as_sf(acacia_melanoxylon_points_gishwati, coords = c("x", "y"), crs = 4326),
  Alnus_acuminata  = st_as_sf(alnus_acuminata_points_gishwati, coords = c("x", "y"), crs = 4326)
)

sf_points_mukura <- list(
  Acacia_mearnsii  = st_as_sf(acacia_mearnsii_points_mukura,  coords = c("x", "y"), crs = 4326),
  Acacia_melanoxylon = st_as_sf(acacia_melanoxylon_points_mukura, coords = c("x", "y"), crs = 4326),
  Alnus_acuminata  = st_as_sf(alnus_acuminata_points_mukura, coords = c("x", "y"), crs = 4326)
)

# Combine into one sf with species column
combine_species_sf <- function(sf_list) {
  bind_rows(
    lapply(names(sf_list), function(sp) {
      sf_list[[sp]] %>% mutate(Species = sp)
    })
  )
}

gishwati_points <- combine_species_sf(sf_points_gishwati)
mukura_points   <- combine_species_sf(sf_points_mukura)

# ======================================================
# 🟢 GISHWATI MAP
# ======================================================
map_gishwati <- ggplot() +
  geom_sf(data = gishwati_buffer, fill = "darkseagreen3", color = "darkgreen", size = 0.6, alpha = 0.4) +
  geom_sf(data = gishwati, fill = "forestgreen", color = "darkgreen", size = 0.7, alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_sf(data = gishwati_points, aes(color = Species), size = 2, alpha = 0.9) +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c(
      "Acacia_mearnsii" = "red",
      "Acacia_melanoxylon" = "orange",
      "Alnus_acuminata" = "blue"
    ),
    name = "Species"
  ) +
  labs(title = "Gishwati Forest: Core, Buffer & Invasive Species Survey Points") +
  annotation_scale(location = "br", width_hint = 0.3) +
  annotation_north_arrow(location = "tr", which_north = "true",
                         style = north_arrow_fancy_orienteering) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "gray85"),
    legend.position = "right"
  )

# Export PDF
ggsave(
  filename = file.path(outdir, "Gishwati_Core_Buffer_Species_Map.pdf"),
  plot = map_gishwati,
  width = 8, height = 6, dpi = 600, useDingbats = FALSE
)



```

```{r}
# ======================================================
# 🟢 MUKURA MAP
# ======================================================
map_mukura <- ggplot() +
  geom_sf(data = mukura_buffer, fill = "darkseagreen3", color = "darkgreen", size = 0.6, alpha = 0.4) +
  geom_sf(data = mukura, fill = "forestgreen", color = "darkgreen", size = 0.7, alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_sf(data = mukura_points, aes(color = Species), size = 2, alpha = 0.9) +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c(
      "Acacia_mearnsii" = "red",
      "Acacia_melanoxylon" = "orange",
      "Alnus_acuminata" = "blue"
    ),
    name = "Species"
  ) +
  labs(title = "Mukura Forest: Core, Buffer & Invasive Species Survey Points") +
  annotation_scale(location = "br", width_hint = 0.3) +
  annotation_north_arrow(location = "tr", which_north = "true",
                         style = north_arrow_fancy_orienteering) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "gray85"),
    legend.position = "right"
  )

# Export PDF
ggsave(
  filename = file.path(outdir, "Mukura_Core_Buffer_Species_Map.pdf"),
  plot = map_mukura,
  width = 8, height = 6, dpi = 600, useDingbats = FALSE
)

```

Deep analysis

```{r}
str(fia_csv)
```

## **Step 1: Define Site Boundaries**

## 

`gishwati.shp`

`mukura.shp`

Use them as the spatial extent for extraction.

**Step 2: Obtain Spatial Datasets**

A. **Soil and Habitat Data**

You need **vector data** (ideally from Rwandan or global sources):
**Soil**: FAO's **Harmonized World Soil Database (HWSD)** or
**SoilGrids** (global, high-resolution)

-   Download: <https://soilgrids.org>

    **Habitat/Land Cover**: **Copernicus Land Cover (100m)** or **ESA
    WorldCover (10m)**: <https://esa-worldcover.org>

    Rwanda-specific land cover: Check **Rwanda Land Management and Use
    Authority (RLMUA)** or Rwanda Spatial Data Portal

    B. **Climate Data**

Use **WorldClim v2.1** for:**Temperature**

**Precipitation**

Resolution: up to 1 km, but can be resampled. Download:
https://www.worldclim.org/data/worldclim21.html

Step 3. Process Data in R

```{r}
library(sf)
library(terra)
library(dplyr)

# Load shapefiles
gishwati <- st_read("path_to/Gishwati_core.shp")
mukura <- st_read("path_to/Mukura_core.shp")
study_area <- st_union(gishwati, mukura)

# Transform to common CRS (e.g., UTM 35S)
study_area <- st_transform(study_area, crs = 32735)

```

A. Download and Clip Soil Raster (e.g., from SoilGrids)

```{r}
# Load a raster soil layer (e.g., organic carbon or texture)
soil_raster <- rast("path_to/soil_data.tif")
soil_raster <- project(soil_raster, gmb_reserve)
soil_crop <- crop(soil_raster, vect(gmb_reserve))
soil_mask <- mask(soil_crop, vect(gmb_reserve))

```

B. **Download and Clip Habitat/Land Cover**

```{r}
landcover <- rast("path_to/landcover.tif")
landcover <- project(landcover, gmb_reserve)
landcover_crop <- crop(landcover, vect(gmb_reserve))
landcover_mask <- mask(landcover_crop, vect(gmb_reserve))

```

C. Download and Clip WorldClim Temperature & Precipitation

```{r}
# Load WorldClim Bio variables
temp <- rast("wc2.1_30s_tavg.tif")  # Mean temp
prec <- rast("wc2.1_30s_prec.tif")  # Precipitation

temp_crop <- crop(temp, vect(study_area))
temp_mask <- mask(temp_crop, vect(study_area))

prec_crop <- crop(prec, vect(study_area))
prec_mask <- mask(prec_crop, vect(study_area))

```

Step 4: Rasterize Vector Soil & Habitat to 20m Resolution

```{r}
# Create a 20x20m template raster
template <- rast(extent(study_area), resolution = 20, crs = crs(gmb_reserve))

# Rasterize a vector soil or habitat shapefile to 20m grid
soil_vector <- st_read("path_to/soil_vector.shp")
habitat_vector <- st_read("path_to/habitat_vector.shp")

soil_raster20 <- rasterize(vect(soil_vector), template, field = "soil_type")
habitat_raster20 <- rasterize(vect(habitat_vector), template, field = "habitat_type")

```

Step 5: Export Processed Rasters or Extract for Plots

```{r}
writeRaster(soil_raster20, "soil_20m.tif", overwrite = TRUE)
writeRaster(habitat_raster20, "habitat_20m.tif", overwrite = TRUE)
writeRaster(temp_mask, "temperature.tif", overwrite = TRUE)
writeRaster(prec_mask, "precipitation.tif", overwrite = TRUE)

```

Or extract data for plot coordinates:

```{r}
fia_coords <- st_as_sf(fia_csv, coords = c("Longitude", "Latitude"), crs = 4326)
fia_coords <- st_transform(fia_coords, crs = 32735)

fia_csv$soil <- terra::extract(soil_raster20, vect(fia_coords))$soil_type
fia_csv$habitat <- terra::extract(habitat_raster20, vect(fia_coords))$habitat_type
fia_csv$temp <- terra::extract(temp_mask, vect(fia_coords))[,2]  # mean temp
fia_csv$prec <- terra::extract(prec_mask, vect(fia_coords))[,2]  # total prec

```

**Updates and provide a way to get this data because will be used as
variables to corrate with invasive disstribution data**

## **Load Required Packages**

```{r}
library(terra)
library(sf)
library(exactextractr)  # For raster value extraction at points

```

## **Import Data**

```{r}
# Park boundaries (merge Gishwati and Mukura if separate)
gishwati <- st_read("gishwati_shapefile.shp")
mukura <- st_read("mukura_shapefile.shp")
park_boundary <- st_union(gishwati, mukura)  # Combined park polygon

# WorldCover land cover raster (download ESA tile for Rwanda)
worldcover <- rast("ESA_WorldCover_2023_Rwanda.tif")

```

## **Reclassify WorldCover Data**

Identify WorldCover codes (e.g., **`10`** = cropland, **`30`** =
herbaceous vegetation/pasture, **`50`** = urban/built-up):

```{r}
# Create masks for target classes
developed <- classify(worldcover, cbind(c(50), 1))  # Built-up areas
pasture_crop <- classify(worldcover, cbind(c(10,30), 1))  # Crops + pasture

```

## **Calculate Distance Rasters**

```{r}
# Distance to nearest developed area
dist_developed <- distance(developed, park_boundary, unit = "m")

# Distance to nearest pasture/crop area
dist_pasture_crop <- distance(pasture_crop, park_boundary, unit = "m")

```

## **Extract Distances for Invasive Species Points**

Assume invasive species data is a **`sf`** object with coordinates:

```{r}
invasive_species <- st_read("invasive_species_occurrences.shp")

# Extract distance values at occurrence points
invasive_species$dist_developed <- exact_extract(
  dist_developed, 
  invasive_species, 
  'mean'  # Use 'mean' if points overlap multiple raster cells
)

invasive_species$dist_pasture_crop <- exact_extract(
  dist_pasture_crop, 
  invasive_species, 
  'mean'
)

```

## **Export for Correlation Analysis**

```{r}
# Save as CSV for statistical software
write.csv(st_drop_geometry(invasive_species), "invasive_species_with_distances.csv")

# Optional: Save distance rasters for spatial modeling
writeRaster(dist_developed, "distance_to_developed.tif")
writeRaster(dist_pasture_crop, "distance_to_pasture_crop.tif")

```

```{r}
plot(gmb_reserve)
```

End

UNESCO CODE

Data preparation, descriptive mapping, site comparisons, and groundwork
for SDM and future climate projections. This will serve as a **core
pipeline** for your invasion ecology analysis of *Acacia mearnsii* and
*Acacia melanoxylon* in the Gishwati-Mukura Biosphere Reserve.

Step 1: Prepare the Data

```{r}
library(dplyr)

# Add presence/absence indicators and species-specific dominance
fia_csv <- fia_csv %>%
  mutate(
    Dominant_Presence = ifelse(Dominant_Species %in% c("Acacia mearnsii", "Acacia melanoxylon"), 1, 0),
    Acacia_mearnsii_dominant = ifelse(Dominant_Species == "Acacia mearnsii", 1, 0),
    Acacia_melanoxylon_dominant = ifelse(Dominant_Species == "Acacia melanoxylon", 1, 0),
    Acacia_mearnsii_presence = ifelse(Species_1_Acacia_mearnsii_Perc > 0, 1, 0),
    Acacia_melanoxylon_presence = ifelse(Species_2_Acacia_melanoxylon_Perc > 0, 1, 0)
  )

```

### Updates Include Data for all species

```{r}
library(dplyr)
fia_csv1 <- fia_csv %>%
  mutate(
    Dominant_Presence = ifelse(Dominant_Species %in% c("Acacia mearnsii", "Acacia melanoxylon", "Alnus acuminata"), 1, 0),
    Acacia_mearnsii_dominant = ifelse(Dominant_Species == "Acacia mearnsii", 1, 0),
    Acacia_melanoxylon_dominant = ifelse(Dominant_Species == "Acacia melanoxylon", 1, 0),
    Alnus_acuminata_dominant = ifelse(Dominant_Species == "Alnus acuminata", 1, 0),
    
    Acacia_mearnsii_presence = ifelse(Species_1_Acacia_mearnsii_Perc > 0, 1, 0),
    Acacia_melanoxylon_presence = ifelse(Species_2_Acacia_melanoxylon_Perc > 0, 1, 0),
    Alnus_acuminata_presence = ifelse(Species_3_Alnus_acuminata_Perc > 0, 1, 0)
  )

```

Summary

```{r}
species_presence_summary <- fia_csv1 %>%
  summarize(
    Acacia_mearnsii_presence_n = sum(Acacia_mearnsii_presence, na.rm = TRUE),
    Acacia_melanoxylon_presence_n = sum(Acacia_melanoxylon_presence, na.rm = TRUE),
    Alnus_acuminata_presence_n = sum(Alnus_acuminata_presence, na.rm = TRUE)
  )

print(species_presence_summary)

```

### Step 2: Descriptive Mapping (Spatial Visualization)

### Map presence (or dominance) for each species across Mukura and Gishwati.

```{r}

library(ggplot2)
library(sf)

# Convert to spatial object (if not yet)
fia_sf <- st_as_sf(fia_csv, coords = c("Central_coordinate_Longitude", "Central_coordinate_Latitude"), crs = 4326)

# Plot Acacia mearnsii presence
Plot_mearnsii<-ggplot() +
  geom_sf(data = gmb_reserve, fill = "gray80", color = "black") +
  geom_sf(data = fia_sf %>% filter(Acacia_mearnsii_presence == 1), aes(color = Site), size = 2) +
  labs(title = "Presence of Acacia mearnsii in Gishwati and Mukura", color = "Site") +
  theme_minimal()+annotation_scale(location = "bl", width_hint = 0.4) +
  annotation_north_arrow(location = "tr", which_north = "true", style = north_arrow_fancy_orienteering) 

print(Plot_mearnsii)

```

```{r}
ggsave(
  filename = "Plot_Acacia_mearnsii_presence.png",
  plot = Plot_mearnsii,
  width = 10, height = 8, units = "in", dpi = 600
)
```

```{r}
# Plot Acacia melanoxylon presence
Plot_melanoxylon<-ggplot() +
  geom_sf(data = gmb_reserve, fill = "gray80", color = "black") +
  geom_sf(data = fia_sf %>% filter(Acacia_melanoxylon_presence == 1), aes(color = Site), size = 2) +
  labs(title = "Presence of Acacia melanoxylon in Gishwati and Mukura", color = "Site") +
  theme_minimal() +annotation_scale(location = "bl", width_hint = 0.4) +
  annotation_north_arrow(location = "tr", which_north = "true", style = north_arrow_fancy_orienteering) 

print(Plot_melanoxylon)
```

```{r}
ggsave(
  filename = "Plot_Acacia_melanoxylon_presence.png",
  plot = Plot_melanoxylon,
  width = 10, height = 8, units = "in", dpi = 600
)
```

```{r}
# Plot Alnus acuminata presence

library(ggplot2)
library(sf)
library(ggspatial)  # For scale bar and north arrow
library(dplyr)

# Convert to sf object
fia_sf <- st_as_sf(fia_csv1, coords = c("Central_coordinate_Longitude", "Central_coordinate_Latitude"), crs = 4326)

# Plot
Plot_acuminata <- ggplot() +
  geom_sf(data = gmb_reserve, fill = "gray80", color = "black") +
  geom_sf(data = fia_sf %>% filter(Alnus_acuminata_presence == 1), aes(color = Site), size = 2) +
  labs(
    title = "Presence of Alnus acuminata in Gishwati and Mukura",
    color = "Site"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  annotation_scale(location = "bl", width_hint = 0.4) +
  annotation_north_arrow(location = "tr", which_north = "true", 
                         style = north_arrow_fancy_orienteering)

# Print the plot
print(Plot_acuminata)

```

```{r}
ggsave(
  filename = "Plot_Alnus_acuminata_presence.png",
  plot = Plot_acuminata,
  width = 10, height = 8, units = "in", dpi = 600
)
```

## Step 3: Comparative Site Analysis

Compare presence, dominance, and abundance of both species across sites.

```{r}
# Summary by site
# Summary by site including Alnus acuminata
summary_table <- fia_csv1 %>%
  group_by(Site) %>%
  summarise(
    Plots_Total = n(),
    
    # Acacia mearnsii
    A_mearnsii_Presence = sum(Acacia_mearnsii_presence, na.rm = TRUE),
    A_mearnsii_Dominant = sum(Acacia_mearnsii_dominant, na.rm = TRUE),
    Mean_A_mearnsii_Percent = mean(Species_1_Acacia_mearnsii_Perc, na.rm = TRUE),
    
    # Acacia melanoxylon
    A_melanoxylon_Presence = sum(Acacia_melanoxylon_presence, na.rm = TRUE),
    A_melanoxylon_Dominant = sum(Acacia_melanoxylon_dominant, na.rm = TRUE),
    Mean_A_melanoxylon_Percent = mean(Species_2_Acacia_melanoxylon_Perc, na.rm = TRUE),

    # Alnus acuminata
    Alnus_acuminata_Presence = sum(Alnus_acuminata_presence, na.rm = TRUE),
    Alnus_acuminata_Dominant = sum(Alnus_acuminata_dominant, na.rm = TRUE),
    Mean_Alnus_acuminata_Percent = mean(Species_3_Alnus_acuminata_Perc, na.rm = TRUE)
  )

# Display summary table
print(summary_table)


```

```{r}
write.csv(summary_table, "Result_summary_table.csv", row.names = FALSE)
```

Reshape for Box

```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)

# Prepare data
plot_data <- summary_table %>%
  select(
    Site,
    Mean_A_mearnsii_Percent,
    Mean_A_melanoxylon_Percent,
    Mean_Alnus_acuminata_Percent,
    A_mearnsii_Dominant,
    A_melanoxylon_Dominant,
    Alnus_acuminata_Dominant
  ) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = starts_with("Mean"),
    names_to = "Species",
    values_to = "Mean_Percent"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    Species = recode(Species,
                     "Mean_A_mearnsii_Percent" = "Acacia mearnsii",
                     "Mean_A_melanoxylon_Percent" = "Acacia melanoxylon",
                     "Mean_Alnus_acuminata_Percent" = "Alnus acuminata"),
    Dominant = case_when(
      Species == "Acacia mearnsii" ~ A_mearnsii_Dominant,
      Species == "Acacia melanoxylon" ~ A_melanoxylon_Dominant,
      Species == "Alnus acuminata" ~ Alnus_acuminata_Dominant
    )
  )

# Bubble plot
Plot_percentage_Cover <- ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = Site, y = Mean_Percent, color = Species, size = Dominant)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.8) +
  scale_size_continuous(name = "Dominant count (n)", range = c(3, 10)) +
  labs(
    title = "Mean Braun-Blanquet % Cover per Species by Site",
    x = "Site", y = "Mean % cover"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

print(Plot_percentage_Cover)

```

```{r}
ggsave(
  filename = "Plot_Percentage_Cover.png",
  plot = Plot_percentage_Cover,
  width = 10, height = 8, units = "in", dpi = 600
)
```

Extract Presence Data Points for Each Species\

```{r}
# Convert to spatial object if not already
#fia_sf <- st_as_sf(fia_csv, coords = c("Central_coordinate_Longitude", "Central_coordinate_Latitude"), crs = 4326)

# Extract presence points for Acacia mearnsii
acacia_mearnsii_points <- fia_sf %>% filter(Acacia_mearnsii_presence == 1)

# Extract presence points for Acacia melanoxylon
acacia_melanoxylon_points <- fia_sf %>% filter(Acacia_melanoxylon_presence == 1)

```

```{r}
# Extract presence points for Alnus acuminata
alnus_acuminata_points <- fia_sf %>% filter(Alnus_acuminata_presence == 1)
```

Or Export These as Shapefiles or CSV for MaxEnt

```{r}
# As shapefile
#st_write(acacia_mearnsii_points, "acacia_mearnsii_points.shp", delete_layer = TRUE)
#st_write(acacia_melanoxylon_points, "acacia_melanoxylon_points.shp", delete_layer = TRUE)

# Or as CSV with coordinates
acacia_mearnsii_points <- st_coordinates(acacia_mearnsii_points) %>% 
  as.data.frame() %>%
  bind_cols(acacia_mearnsii_points %>% st_drop_geometry())

write.csv(acacia_mearnsii_points, "acacia_mearnsii_points.csv", row.names = FALSE)

acacia_melanoxylon_points<- st_coordinates(acacia_melanoxylon_points) %>% 
  as.data.frame() %>%
  bind_cols(acacia_melanoxylon_points %>% st_drop_geometry())

write.csv(acacia_melanoxylon_points, "acacia_melanoxylon_points.csv", row.names = FALSE)
```

```{r}
alnus_acuminata_points<- st_coordinates(alnus_acuminata_points) %>% 
  as.data.frame() %>%
  bind_cols(alnus_acuminata_points %>% st_drop_geometry())

write.csv(alnus_acuminata_points, "alnus_acuminata_points.csv", row.names = FALSE)
```

Download trails/roads data from OpenStreetMap\

```{r}
# -------------------------------------------
# LOAD LIBRARIES
# -------------------------------------------

library(osmdata)
library(sf)
library(terra)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyverse)

# -------------------------------------------
# SET CRS AND DIRECTORY
# -------------------------------------------
rwanda_crs <- 4326
shapefile_dir <- "/Users/blaise/Rwanda_National Parks/Code_Species_Modeling/Gishwati_Mukura"

# -------------------------------------------
# READ AND MERGE SHAPEFILES (Gishwati, Mukura)
# -------------------------------------------
gishwati <- st_read(file.path(shapefile_dir, "Gishwati_core.shp")) %>%
  st_transform(crs = rwanda_crs) %>%
  mutate(source = "Gishwati_core") %>%
  select(source, geometry)

mukura <- st_read(file.path(shapefile_dir, "Mukura_core.shp")) %>%
  st_transform(crs = rwanda_crs) %>%
  mutate(source = "Mukura_core") %>%
  select(source, geometry)

gishwati_buffer <- st_read(file.path(shapefile_dir, "Gishwati_buffer.shp")) %>%
  st_transform(crs = rwanda_crs) %>%
  mutate(source = "Gishwati_buffer") %>%
  select(source, geometry)

mukura_buffer <- st_read(file.path(shapefile_dir, "Mukura_buffer.shp")) %>%
  st_transform(crs = rwanda_crs) %>%
  mutate(source = "Mukura_buffer") %>%
  select(source, geometry)

# Merge all
gmb_reserve <- rbind(gishwati, mukura, gishwati_buffer, mukura_buffer)


```

```{r}

# -------------------------------------------
# COMBINE FOR REFERENCE RASTER
# -------------------------------------------
gmb_reserve <- rbind(gishwati, mukura, gishwati_buffer, mukura_buffer)
gmb_vect <- vect(gmb_reserve)
gmb_vect <- project(gmb_vect, dem_rwanda2)

# -------------------------------------------
# REFERENCE RASTER
# -------------------------------------------
ref_raster <- mask(crop(dem_rwanda2, gmb_vect), gmb_vect)

# -------------------------------------------
# DEFINE INDIVIDUAL BUFFERS AS sf_area
# -------------------------------------------
sf_area_gishwati <- gishwati_buffer
sf_area_mukura <- mukura_buffer


```

```{r}
get_osm_distance_raster <- function(sf_area, key = "highway", values = NULL, ref_raster, name = "osm_distance") {
  # Convert area to WGS84 for Overpass API
  sf_area_latlon <- st_transform(sf_area, 4326)
  bbox <- st_bbox(sf_area_latlon)

  # Create valid bbox vector
  bbox_vector <- c(
    min_lon = bbox["xmin"],
    min_lat = bbox["ymin"],
    max_lon = bbox["xmax"],
    max_lat = bbox["ymax"]
  )

  # OSM query
  query <- opq(bbox = bbox_vector) %>%
    add_osm_feature(key = key, value = values)

  # Download and handle errors
  osm <- tryCatch(
    osmdata_sf(query),
    error = function(e) {
      warning(paste("OSM query failed for", name, ":", e$message))
      return(NULL)
    }
  )

  # Check and process lines
  if (is.null(osm) || is.null(osm$osm_lines)) {
    warning(paste("No OSM lines found for", name))
    return(NULL)
  }

  # Transform OSM lines to match sf_area CRS
  osm_lines <- st_transform(osm$osm_lines, st_crs(sf_area))

  # Intersect with area
  osm_lines <- st_intersection(osm_lines, sf_area)
  if (nrow(osm_lines) == 0) {
    warning(paste("No intersecting lines for", name))
    return(NULL)
  }

  # Convert to SpatVector and match CRS with raster
  osm_vect <- vect(osm_lines)
  osm_vect <- terra::project(osm_vect, ref_raster)

  # Create distance raster
  dist_raster <- terra::distance(ref_raster, osm_vect)
  names(dist_raster) <- name
  return(dist_raster)
}

# -------------------------------------------
# CALL FUNCTION FOR EACH AREA
# -------------------------------------------
trails_gishwati <- get_osm_distance_raster(
  sf_area = sf_area_gishwati,
  key = "highway",
  values = c("motorway", "primary", "secondary", "tertiary", 
             "residential", "track", "path", "footway", "footpath", "cycleway"),
  ref_raster = ref_raster,
  name = "dist_gishwati_trails"
)

trails_mukura <- get_osm_distance_raster(
  sf_area = sf_area_mukura,
  key = "highway",
  values = c("motorway", "primary", "secondary", "tertiary", 
             "residential", "track", "path", "footway", "footpath", "cycleway"),
  ref_raster = ref_raster,
  name = "dist_mukura_trails"
)

```

```{r}
# Validate rasters
valid_rasters <- list()

if (inherits(trails_gishwati, "SpatRaster") && nlyr(trails_gishwati) > 0) {
  valid_rasters[[length(valid_rasters) + 1]] <- trails_gishwati
}
if (inherits(trails_mukura, "SpatRaster") && nlyr(trails_mukura) > 0) {
  valid_rasters[[length(valid_rasters) + 1]] <- trails_mukura
}

# Assuming valid_rasters has 2 or more rasters all aligned:
if (length(valid_rasters) == 0) {
  stop("No valid trail distance rasters found.")
} else if (length(valid_rasters) == 1) {
  dist_combined <- valid_rasters[[1]]
} else {
  # Stack rasters
  rast_stack <- rast(valid_rasters)
  # Calculate cell-wise min
  dist_combined <- app(rast_stack, fun = min, na.rm = TRUE)
}

names(dist_combined) <- "dist_trails_all"

```

```{r}
plot(dist_combined)
```

```{r}

# -------------------------------------------
# OPTIONAL: SAVE OUTPUT
# -------------------------------------------
writeRaster(dist_combined, "dist_trails_all.tif", overwrite = TRUE)

```

```{r}

sf_area_gishwati_proj <- st_transform(sf_area_gishwati, crs(dist_combined))

masked_raster <- terra::mask(dist_combined, vect(sf_area_gishwati_proj))

plot(masked_raster)

```

```{r}
# Transform polygons to match raster CRS
sf_area_gishwati_proj <- st_transform(sf_area_gishwati, crs(dist_combined))
sf_area_mukura_proj   <- st_transform(sf_area_mukura, crs(dist_combined))

# Mask each raster with its respective polygon
masked_gishwati <- terra::mask(trails_gishwati, vect(sf_area_gishwati_proj))
masked_mukura   <- terra::mask(trails_mukura, vect(sf_area_mukura_proj))

# Combine masked rasters (stack + cell-wise min)
masked_stack <- rast(list(masked_gishwati, masked_mukura))
combined_masked <- app(masked_stack, fun = min, na.rm = TRUE)

names(combined_masked) <- "dist_trails_masked"

# Plot final combined masked raster
plot(combined_masked, main = "Combined Masked Trail Distance Raster")

```

UPDATE

```{r}
# Transform polygons to match raster CRS
sf_area_gishwati_proj <- st_transform(sf_area_gishwati, crs(dist_combined))
sf_area_mukura_proj   <- st_transform(sf_area_mukura, crs(dist_combined))

# Mask each raster with its respective polygon
masked_gishwati <- terra::mask(trails_gishwati, vect(sf_area_gishwati_proj))
masked_mukura   <- terra::mask(trails_mukura, vect(sf_area_mukura_proj))

# Combine masked rasters (stack + cell-wise min)
masked_stack <- rast(list(masked_gishwati, masked_mukura))
combined_masked <- app(masked_stack, fun = min, na.rm = TRUE)
names(combined_masked) <- "dist_trails_masked"

# Plot with custom extent
plot(
  combined_masked,
  main = "Combined Masked Trail Distance", # Slightly refined title
  xlab = "Longitude", # Explicitly label X-axis
  ylab = "Latitude",  # Explicitly label Y-axis
  xlim = c(29.32, 29.57), # Keep user's specified limits
  ylim = c(-2.05, -1.79), # Adjusted slightly to ensure full visibility if needed
)
```

```{r}
# Export combined_masked raster to GeoTIFF
terra::writeRaster(
  combined_masked,
  filename = "combined_masked_distance.tif",
  filetype = "GTiff",
  overwrite = TRUE
)

```

```{r}
hist(values(combined_masked), main = "Histogram of Trail Distances", xlab = "Distance (m)")

```

```{r}
near_trail <- combined_masked < 500
plot(near_trail, main = "Areas Within 500m of Trails")


```

DEM

```{r}
library(terra)  # Or use 'raster' if preferred

# Load the mean elevation raster from each tile
dem1 <- rast("/Users/blaise/Rwanda_National Parks/Code_Species_Modeling/Gishwati_Mukura/GMTED2010S10E000_300/10s000e_20101117_gmted_mea300.tif")  # covers west Rwanda
dem2 <- rast("/Users/blaise/Rwanda_National Parks/Code_Species_Modeling/Gishwati_Mukura/GMTED2010S10E030_300/10s030e_20101117_gmted_mea300.tif")  # covers east Rwanda

# Combine (mosaic) the rasters into one
dem_combined <- mosaic(dem1, dem2, fun = "mean")  # 'mean' handles overlap if any

# Crop to Rwanda boundary (optional)
rwanda_shp <- vect("/Users/blaise/Rwanda_National Parks/Code_Species_Modeling/Gishwati_Mukura/Rwanda-shapfile/Data_GishwatiMukura/National boundary_Rwanda.shp")
dem_rwanda <- crop(dem_combined, rwanda_shp)
dem_rwanda <- mask(dem_rwanda, rwanda_shp)

# Plot
plot(dem_rwanda, main = "Elevation Map of Rwanda")

```

```{r}
# Derive slope (in degrees)
library(terra)

# Example: Assuming you already created `dem_rwanda` by merging the two tiles
slope <- terrain(dem_rwanda, v = "slope", unit = "degrees")


# Plot
plot(slope, main = "Slope Map of Rwanda")

```

Export DEM raster

```{r}

# Export DEM raster correctly using terra
writeRaster(
  dem_rwanda,
  filename = file.path(shapefile_dir, "rwanda_dem_combined.tif"),
  filetype = "GTiff",
  overwrite = TRUE)



# Export slope raster
writeRaster(
  slope,
  filename = file.path(shapefile_dir, "rwanda_slope.tif"),
  filetype = "GTiff",
  overwrite = TRUE
)

```

LAND COVER 2020

```{r}
# Load terra package
library(terra)

# Set working directory or folder path
land_cover_dir <- "/Users/blaise/Rwanda_National Parks/Code_Species_Modeling/Gishwati_Mukura"

# Load the two land cover tiles
lc1 <- rast(file.path(land_cover_dir, "2020_00N_030E.tif"))
lc2 <- rast(file.path(land_cover_dir, "2020_00N_020E.tif"))

# Merge the two tiles into one raster
land_cover_combined <- mosaic(lc1, lc2)


# Optional: Set CRS to match your study area (if needed)
#crs(land_cover_combined) <- crs(your_vector_data)

# Export the merged land cover raster
writeRaster(
  land_cover_combined,
  filename = file.path(land_cover_dir, "rwanda_land_cover_combined.tif"),
  filetype = "GTiff",
  overwrite = TRUE
)

```

```{r}

# Then run this:
plot(land_cover_combined, main = "Land Cover Combined")

```

```{r}
plot(land_cover_combined, main = "Land Cover Combined")
Land_cover_rwanda <- crop(land_cover_combined, rwanda_shp)
Land_cover_rwanda <- mask(land_cover_combined, rwanda_shp)

```

```{r}
plot(Land_cover_rwanda, main = "Land Cover Combined_Rwanda")
```

Crop the raster to Rwanda's bounding box

```{r}
# Crop raster to the Rwanda extent
land_cover_rwanda_crop <- crop(Land_cover_rwanda, ext(28.8, 30.9, -2.9, -1.0))

```

Save the cropped raster

```{r}
# Save the cropped raster
writeRaster(
  land_cover_rwanda_crop,
  filename = "/Users/blaise/Rwanda_National Parks/Code_Species_Modeling/Gishwati_Mukura/land_cover_rwanda_cropped.tif",
  filetype = "GTiff",
  overwrite = TRUE
)

```

```{r}
plot(land_cover_rwanda_crop, main = "Land Cover - Rwanda Cropped")

```

### Identify Raster Values

First, inspect the **unique values** in my land cover rster

```{r}
unique(values(land_cover_rwanda_crop))

```

### Define Classification Table

You can define a **lookup table** (based on FAO LCCS or the specific
product you're using — like ESA or Hansen). Below is an **example
classification** used in many 17-class FAO-based datasets:

```{r}
rwanda_landcover_classes <- data.frame(
  code = c(
    0:24,                 # Short veg/desert/semi-arid
    25:48, 125:148,       # Tree cover (both green and blue ranges)
    100:124,              # Sparse vegetation
    200:207,              # Water bodies
    244,                  # Cropland
    250,                  # Built-up
    254,                  # Ocean
    241                   # Snow/ice
  ),
  class = c(
    rep("Short/Sparse Vegetation", 25),
    rep("Tree Cover", length(c(25:48, 125:148))),
    rep("Sparse Vegetation", 25),
    rep("Open Water", 8),
    "Cropland",
    "Built-up",
    "Ocean",
    "Snow/Ice"
  )
)

```

Join Classification to Raster (as factor)

```{r}
# Convert raster values to a factor with levels
library(terra)

# Convert to factor raster
land_cover_rwanda_crop <- as.factor(land_cover_rwanda_crop)

# Link classification labels
levels(land_cover_rwanda_crop)[[1]] <- rwanda_landcover_classes


```

Plot with Class Labels

```{r}

plot(land_cover_rwanda_crop, main = "Land Cover Rwanda (GLCLU Group Classes)")

```

```{r}
writeRaster(land_cover_rwanda_crop, "Land_Cover_Rwanda_Classified.tif", overwrite = TRUE)

```

Step-by-step Code to Load and Extract BIOCLIM Layers 2021-2040\

```{r}
library(terra)

# Load the multi-band bioclim .tif file
bioclim_future <- rast("/Users/blaise/Rwanda_National Parks/Code_Species_Modeling/Gishwati_Mukura/wc2.1_30s_bioc_MPI-ESM1-2-HR_ssp245_2021-2040.tif")

# Check how many layers (bands) it contains
nlyr(bioclim_future)  # should return 19

# Assign proper names to each band (BIO1 to BIO19)
names(bioclim_future) <- paste0("BIO", 1:19)
```

```{r}

# Extract specific variables, for example:
bio1 <- bioclim_future[[1]]   # Annual Mean Temperature
bio12 <- bioclim_future[[12]] # Annual Precipitation
bio4 <- bioclim_future[[4]]   #Temperature Seasonality
bio5 <- bioclim_future[[5]]   #Temperature of Warmest Month
bio8 <- bioclim_future[[8]]   #Mean Temperature of Wettest Quarter
bio14 <- bioclim_future[[14]] #Precipitation of Driest Month
bio15 <- bioclim_future[[15]] #Precipitation Seasonality
bio18 <- bioclim_future[[18]] #Precipitation of Warmest Quarter
bio6 <- bioclim_future[[6]] #Min temperature of coldest month
bio10 <- bioclim_future[[10]]# Mean temperature of
#warmest quarter
# Plot an example
#plot(bio1, main = "BIO1 - Annual Mean Temperature (2021–2040)")

# Save specific variables if needed
writeRaster(bio1, "bio1_2021_2040.tif", overwrite = TRUE)
writeRaster(bio12, "bio12_2021_2040.tif", overwrite = TRUE)
writeRaster(bio4, "bio4_2021_2040.tif", overwrite = TRUE)
writeRaster(bio5, "bio5_2021_2040.tif", overwrite = TRUE)
writeRaster(bio8, "bio8_2021_2040.tif", overwrite = TRUE)
writeRaster(bio14, "bio14_2021_2040.tif", overwrite = TRUE)
writeRaster(bio15, "bio15_2021_2040.tif", overwrite = TRUE)
writeRaster(bio18, "bio18_2021_2040.tif", overwrite = TRUE)
writeRaster(bio6, "bio6_2021_2040.tif", overwrite = TRUE)
writeRaster(bio10, "bio10_2021_2040.tif", overwrite = TRUE)
```

### [World reference base for soil resources 2006](https://www.fao.org/fileadmin/templates/nr/images/resources/pdf_documents/wrb2007_red.pdf)

```{r}
soil2 <- rast("/Users/blaise/Rwanda_National Parks/Code_Species_Modeling/Gishwati_Mukura/soilRwanda2.tif")
```

```{r}
plot(soil2, main = "Rwanda soil")
```

```{r}

soil_rwanda <- crop(soil2, rwanda_shp)
soil_rwanda <- mask(soil2, rwanda_shp)
plot(soil_rwanda, main = "Rwanda soil")
```

```{r}
unique(values(soil_rwanda))

```

```{r}
writeRaster(soil_rwanda, "soilrwanda2.tif", overwrite = TRUE)
```

```{r}
# Example: Reclassify integer raster to RSG names in R
library(terra)

# Load raster
soil_rwanda2 <- rast("soilrwanda2.tif")

# Create reclassification matrix and convert to data.frame
reclass_matrix <- data.frame(
  value = c(0, 3, 4, 6, 10, 11, 12, 14, 15, 16, 17, 18, 19, 20, 22, 24, 28, 29),
  WRB   = c("Histosols", "Cryosols", "Leptosols", "Vertisols", "Podzols", "Plinthosols",
            "Planosols", "Nitisols", "Ferralsols", "Chernozems", "Kastanozems", "Phaeozems",
            "Umbrisols", "Durisols", "Calcisols", "Alisols", "Arenosols", "Cambisols")
)




```

```{r}
# Convert to factor
soil_rwanda2 <- as.factor(soil_rwanda2)

# Assign the classification labels
levels(soil_rwanda2) <- list(WRB = reclass_matrix$WRB)
levels(soil_rwanda2)[[1]]$value <- reclass_matrix$value
```

```{r}
plot(soil_rwanda2, main = "Rwanda soil")
```

```{r}
writeRaster(soil_rwanda2, "soilrwanda2_class2006.tif", overwrite = TRUE)
```

```{r}
soil <- rast("/Users/blaise/Rwanda_National Parks/Code_Species_Modeling/Gishwati_Mukura/Soil_Rwanda.tif")
```

```{r}
plot(soil, main = "Rwanda soil")
```

```{r}
soilflip <- flip(soil, direction = "vertical")
```

```{r}
plot(soilflip, main = "Rwanda soil")
```

```{r}
library(terra)
library(dplyr)

# Load raster
#soil <- rast("/Users/blaise/Rwanda_National Parks/Code_Species_Modeling/Gishwati_Mukura/Soil_Rwanda.tif")

# RGB reference map
soil_rgb_map <- data.frame(
  R = c(219, 144, 216, 130, 181, 124, 115, 166, 255, 147, 167, 110, 255),
  G = c(209, 114, 205, 96, 159, 87, 76, 140, 210, 117, 144, 70, 255),
  B = c(190, 54, 183, 30, 119, 22, 0, 93, 122, 59, 100, 0, 255),
  SoilUnit = c("Alfisols", "Andisols", "Aridisols", "Entisols", "Gelisols",
                "Histosols", "Inceptisols", "Mollisols", "Oxisols",
                "Spodosols", "Ultisols", "Vertisols", "Water/NoData"),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

# Extract RGB bands
r <- soil[[1]]
g <- soil[[2]]
b <- soil[[3]]

# Combine RGB values into matrix
rgb_vals <- cbind(values(r), values(g), values(b))

# Function to match RGB to soil unit
match_rgb_to_soil <- function(rgb_pixel, ref_map) {
  dists <- sqrt(rowSums((t(t(ref_map[, 1:3]) - rgb_pixel))^2))
  return(which.min(dists))  # Return index for factor raster
}

# Apply classification
soil_indices <- apply(rgb_vals, 1, match_rgb_to_soil, ref_map = soil_rgb_map)

# Create empty single-layer raster
soil_class_raster <- setValues(rast(soilflip, nlyrs = 1), soil_indices)

# Convert to factor and assign levels
soil_factor <- as.factor(soil_class_raster)
levels(soil_factor) <- data.frame(ID = 1:nrow(soil_rgb_map), SoilUnit = soil_rgb_map$SoilUnit)

# Optional: Set layer name
names(soil_factor) <- "SoilType"



```

```{r}
# Plot to verify
soil_factor<-plot(soil_factor, col = rgb(soil_rgb_map$R, soil_rgb_map$G, soil_rgb_map$B, maxColorValue = 255), 
     main = "Classified Soil (Factor Raster)", legend = FALSE)
legend("bottomleft", legend = soil_rgb_map$SoilUnit,
       fill = rgb(soil_rgb_map$R, soil_rgb_map$G, soil_rgb_map$B, maxColorValue = 255),
       cex = 0.8, bty = "n")
```

```{r}
# Flip the raster vertically using terra
soil_factor_flipped <- terra::flip(soil_factor, direction = "vertical")

# Plot the flipped raster with colors
plot(soil_factor_flipped, 
     col = rgb(soil_rgb_map$R, soil_rgb_map$G, soil_rgb_map$B, maxColorValue = 255), 
     main = "Classified Soil (Flipped)", 
     legend = FALSE, 
     axes = FALSE, 
     box = FALSE)

# Add legend (unchanged)
legend("bottomleft", 
       legend = soil_rgb_map$SoilUnit,
       fill = rgb(soil_rgb_map$R, soil_rgb_map$G, soil_rgb_map$B, maxColorValue = 255),
       cex = 0.8, bty = "n")

```

```{r}
unique(values(soil_factor_flipped))
```

```{r}
# Export as GeoTIFF
writeRaster(
  soil_factor,
  filename = "/Users/blaise/Rwanda_National Parks/Code_Species_Modeling/Gishwati_Mukura/Classified_Soil_RwandaUSDA.tif",
  datatype = "INT1U",  # Unsigned 8-bit integer for categorical data
  overwrite = TRUE
)
```

```{r}
library(sf)

# Reproject the gmb_reserve (sf object) to match the raster CRS
gmb_reserve_proj <- st_transform(gmb_reserve, crs(dem_rwanda2))

```

```{r}
library(terra)

# Convert sf to SpatVector for terra operations
gmb_reserve_vect <- vect(gmb_reserve_proj)


```

```{r}
plot(gmb_reserve_vect  , main = "Study area")
```

```{r}
dem_crop_tmp <- crop(dem_rwanda2, gmb_reserve_vect)
plot(dem_crop_tmp)
plot(gmb_reserve_vect, add=TRUE, border="red")

```

```{r}
library(terra)

# Get polygon extent and expand it by a small buffer
poly_ext <- ext(gmb_reserve_vect)
expand_dist <- 0.01  # degrees; adjust if needed

new_ext <- ext(
  poly_ext$xmin - expand_dist, 
  poly_ext$xmax + expand_dist,
  poly_ext$ymin - expand_dist, 
  poly_ext$ymax + expand_dist
)

# Crop raster to the expanded extent
dem_crop_tmp <- crop(dem_rwanda2, new_ext)

# Plot to check
plot(dem_crop_tmp, main = "DEM cropped with expanded extent")
plot(gmb_reserve_vect, add = TRUE, border = "red", lwd = 2)

```

```{r}
dem_crop <- mask(dem_crop_tmp, gmb_reserve_vect)
plot(dem_crop, main = "DEM masked to polygon")
plot(gmb_reserve_vect, add = TRUE, border = "blue", lwd = 2)

```

```{r}
# Crop and mask each raster to the study area

library(terra)

# Define a small buffer (adjust based on your CRS units, here in degrees)
expand_dist <- 0.01  

# Get polygon extent and expand it
poly_ext <- ext(gmb_reserve_vect)
new_ext <- ext(
  poly_ext$xmin - expand_dist, 
  poly_ext$xmax + expand_dist,
  poly_ext$ymin - expand_dist, 
  poly_ext$ymax + expand_dist
)

# Function to crop and mask with expanded extent
crop_mask_expanded <- function(rast, vect_poly, extent_expanded) {
  cropped <- crop(rast, extent_expanded)
  masked <- mask(cropped, vect_poly)
  return(masked)
}

# Apply to all rasters
dem_crop <- crop_mask_expanded(dem_rwanda2, gmb_reserve_vect, new_ext)
slope_crop <- crop_mask_expanded(slope, gmb_reserve_vect, new_ext)

soil_crop <- crop_mask_expanded(soil2006, gmb_reserve_vect, new_ext)


```

```{r}
bio1_crop <- crop_mask_expanded(bio1, gmb_reserve_vect, new_ext)
bio12_crop <- crop_mask_expanded(bio12, gmb_reserve_vect, new_ext)
bio4_crop <- crop_mask_expanded(bio4, gmb_reserve_vect, new_ext)
bio5_crop <- crop_mask_expanded(bio5, gmb_reserve_vect, new_ext)
bio8_crop <- crop_mask_expanded(bio8, gmb_reserve_vect, new_ext)
bio14_crop <- crop_mask_expanded(bio14, gmb_reserve_vect, new_ext)
bio15_crop <- crop_mask_expanded(bio15, gmb_reserve_vect, new_ext)
bio18_crop <- crop_mask_expanded(bio18, gmb_reserve_vect, new_ext)

bio6_crop <- crop_mask_expanded(bio6, gmb_reserve_vect, new_ext)

bio10_crop <- crop_mask_expanded(bio10, gmb_reserve_vect, new_ext)
```

UPDATES HARMONIZATION FOR CURRENT CLIMATE

**Project all rasters and vector data to a suitable CRS in meters**

Choose a projected CRS for Rwanda — e.g., **UTM Zone 35S** (EPSG: 32735)

```{r}
# Choose appropriate projected CRS for Rwanda (you can also use EPSG:32735)
target_crs <- "EPSG:32735"

# Reproject the vector (study area)
gmb_reserve_proj <- project(gmb_reserve_vect, target_crs)

# Reproject DEM before cropping
dem_proj <- project(dem_rwanda2, target_crs)

# Now crop and mask DEM using projected vector
dem_crop_proj <- crop(dem_proj, gmb_reserve_proj) %>%
  mask(gmb_reserve_proj)

```

```{r}
plot(gmb_reserve_proj)
```

Create a proper 20m resolution raster template

```{r}
# Create blank raster template at 20x20 meters using projected extent
template_20m <- rast(
  extent = ext(gmb_reserve_proj),
  resolution = 20,
  crs = target_crs
)

```

#### **Resample all rasters (after projection + crop):**For continuous layers: Resample for future

```{r}
# === FUTURE BIOCLIMATIC VARIABLES ===
bio1_rs_future   <- resample(project(bio1_crop,   target_crs), template_20m, method = "bilinear")
bio4_rs_future   <- resample(project(bio4_crop,   target_crs), template_20m, method = "bilinear")
bio5_rs_future   <- resample(project(bio5_crop,   target_crs), template_20m, method = "bilinear")
bio6_rs_future   <- resample(project(bio6_crop,   target_crs), template_20m, method = "bilinear")
bio8_rs_future   <- resample(project(bio8_crop,   target_crs), template_20m, method = "bilinear")
bio10_rs_future  <- resample(project(bio10_crop,  target_crs), template_20m, method = "bilinear")
bio12_rs_future  <- resample(project(bio12_crop,  target_crs), template_20m, method = "bilinear")
bio14_rs_future  <- resample(project(bio14_crop,  target_crs), template_20m, method = "bilinear")
bio15_rs_future  <- resample(project(bio15_crop,  target_crs), template_20m, method = "bilinear")
bio18_rs_future  <- resample(project(bio18_crop,  target_crs), template_20m, method = "bilinear")

```

```{r}
Land_cover_2000_2020_crop <- crop_mask_expanded(land_cover_change_rwanda, gmb_reserve_vect, new_ext)# used landcover that was in enviironment after classification
```

```{r}
# === TOPOGRAPHIC AND LANDSCAPE VARIABLES ===
dem_rs        <- resample(dem_crop_proj, template_20m, method = "bilinear")
slope_rs      <- resample(project(slope_crop,      target_crs), template_20m, method = "bilinear")
landcover_rs  <- resample(project(Land_cover_2000_2020_crop,  target_crs), template_20m, method = "near")     # categorical
soil_rs       <- resample(project(soil_crop,       target_crs), template_20m, method = "near")      # categorical double ckeck the value if NO NaN

```

```{r}
plot(landcover_rs)
```

```{r}
# Keep only desired classes, set others (e.g., NaN) to NA
soil_rs <- classify(soil_rs, rcl = matrix(c(-Inf, -1, NA, 1, 2, NA, 4, 5, NA, 7, Inf, NA), ncol=3, byrow=TRUE))
```

```{r}
unique(values(soil_rs))
```

```{r}
# Remove any cells that are not part of your valid values
valid_vals <- c(0, 3, 6)  # <- Your target values

soil_rs <- subst(soil_rs, setdiff(unique(values(soil_rs)), valid_vals), NA)

```

```{r}
# Convert to factor
soil_rs_f <- as.factor(soil_rs)

# Filter reclass_matrix to only include values present in raster
used_reclass <- reclass_matrix[reclass_matrix$value %in% unique(values(soil_rs)), ]

# Assign labels
levels(soil_rs_f)[[1]] <- used_reclass

# Plot
plot(soil_rs_f, main = "Soil Study Area by WRB Group")


```

```{r}
library(terra)

# 1. Define target CRS (Rwanda UTM zone 35S)
rwanda_crs <- "EPSG:32735"

# 2. Project vector to match raster CRS (WGS84)
gmb_reserve_vect <- project(gmb_reserve_vect, crs(bio1_cu))

# 3. Create a common expanded extent from the reserve (in raster CRS)
#new_ext <- ext(gmb_reserve_vect_ll) + 0.5  # buffer by 0.5 degree, adjust as needed

# 4. Define custom crop + mask function (expand + crop + mask)
crop_mask_expanded <- function(raster_layer, vector_layer, extent_obj) {
  cropped <- crop(raster_layer, extent_obj)
  masked  <- mask(cropped, vector_layer)
  return(masked)
}

# 5. Crop and mask all bioclimatic variables
bio1_crop_cu  <- crop_mask_expanded(bio1_cu,  gmb_reserve_vect, new_ext)
bio4_crop_cu  <- crop_mask_expanded(bio4_cu,  gmb_reserve_vect, new_ext)
bio5_crop_cu  <- crop_mask_expanded(bio5_cu,  gmb_reserve_vect, new_ext)
bio6_crop_cu  <- crop_mask_expanded(bio6_cu,  gmb_reserve_vect, new_ext)
bio8_crop_cu  <- crop_mask_expanded(bio8_cu,  gmb_reserve_vect, new_ext)
bio10_crop_cu <- crop_mask_expanded(bio10_cu, gmb_reserve_vect, new_ext)
bio12_crop_cu <- crop_mask_expanded(bio12_cu, gmb_reserve_vect, new_ext)
bio14_crop_cu <- crop_mask_expanded(bio14_cu, gmb_reserve_vect, new_ext)
bio15_crop_cu <- crop_mask_expanded(bio15_cu, gmb_reserve_vect, new_ext)
bio18_crop_cu <- crop_mask_expanded(bio18_cu, gmb_reserve_vect, new_ext)


```

### Why `(value - 127)/127`?

If the raw file is stored as `Byte (0–255)`:

-   0 → −1

    127 → 0

    255 → +1

That’s a simple linear transformation from one range to another:
NDVIscaled=NDVIbyte255×(1−(−1))+(−1)=NDVIbyte255×2−1NDVI\_{scaled} =
\frac{NDVI_{byte}}{255} \times (1 - (-1)) + (-1) =
\frac{NDVI_{byte}}{255} \times 2 -
1NDVIscaled​=255NDVIbyte​​×(1−(−1))+(−1)=255NDVIbyte​​×2−1

When you rearrange, `(value - 127)/127` is just an approximation of the
same mapping, centered at 127.

```{r}
library(terra)



# Rescale from 0–255 -> -1–1
ndvi_scaled <- (ndvi_raster - 127) / 127

# Check new range
global(ndvi_scaled, range)



```

```{r}
out_path <- "/Users/blaise/Rwanda_National Parks/Code_Species_Modeling/Gishwati_Mukura/Sentinel2_L1C_NDVI_scaled.tif"
writeRaster(ndvi_scaled, out_path, overwrite = TRUE)

```

```{r}
# Check CRS of both
crs(ndvi_scaled)
crs(gmb_reserve_vect)
```

```{r}
ndvi_single <- ndvi_raster[[1]]  # pick first band
ndvi_scaled <- (ndvi_single - 127) / 127
plot(ndvi_scaled)
global(ndvi_scaled, range)

```

```{r}
# Save the scaled raster to a new file
out_path <- "/Users/blaise/Rwanda_National Parks/Code_Species_Modeling/Gishwati_Mukura/Sentinel2_L1C_NDVI_scaled1.tif"
writeRaster(ndvi_scaled, out_path, overwrite = TRUE)
```

The normalized difference vegetation index is a simple, but effective
index for quantifying green vegetation. It is a measure of the state of
vegetation health based on how plants reflect light at certain
wavelengths. The value range of the NDVI is -1 to 1. Negative values of
NDVI (values approaching -1) correspond to water. Values close to zero
(-0.1to 0.1) generally correspond to barren areas of rock, sand, or
snow. Low, positive values represent shrub and grassland (approximately
0.2 to 0.4), while high values indicate temperate and tropical
rainforests (values approaching 1).

```{r}
# Reproject vector if CRS mismatch
gmb_reserve_vecto <- project(gmb_reserve_vect, crs(ndvi_single))

# Crop and mask
ndvi_crope <- crop(ndvi_single, gmb_reserve_vecto)
ndvi_crope <- mask(ndvi_crope, gmb_reserve_vecto)

plot(ndvi_crope, main = "NDVI Cropped to Study Area")
```

```{r}
library(terra)
library(sf)

# Assuming ndvi_single is your main NDVI raster
# And gmb_reserve_vect is your study area vector

# Reproject vector to match NDVI raster CRS
#gmb_reserve_vect <- project(gmb_reserve_vect, crs(ndvi_single))

# Convert to sf for filtering
gmb_reserve_sf <- st_as_sf(gmb_reserve_vecto)

# Check attribute names to identify grouping field
#names(gmb_reserve_sf)

library(terra)
library(sf)

# Convert SpatVector to sf
gmb_reserve_sf <- st_as_sf(gmb_reserve_vecto)

# --- Fix invalid geometries ---
gmb_reserve_sf <- st_make_valid(gmb_reserve_sf)

# Optional: simplify tiny geometry issues
gmb_reserve_sf <- st_buffer(gmb_reserve_sf, dist = 0)

# ---- Split by site ----
gishwati_sf <- subset(gmb_reserve_sf, grepl("Gishwati", gmb_reserve_sf$source, ignore.case = TRUE))
mukura_sf   <- subset(gmb_reserve_sf, grepl("Mukura", gmb_reserve_sf$source, ignore.case = TRUE))

# ---- Union again ----
gishwati_uniona <- st_union(gishwati_sf)
mukura_uniona   <- st_union(mukura_sf)



# Convert back to SpatVector for terra
gishwati_vecti <- vect(gishwati_uniona)
mukura_vecti   <- vect(mukura_uniona)

# ---- Crop & Mask NDVI for each site ----
ndvi_gishwati <- crop(ndvi_single, gishwati_vecti)
ndvi_gishwati <- mask(ndvi_gishwati, gishwati_vecti)

ndvi_mukura <- crop(ndvi_single, mukura_vecti)
ndvi_mukura <- mask(ndvi_mukura, mukura_vecti)

# ---- Plot ----
par(mfrow = c(1, 2))
plot(ndvi_gishwati, main = "NDVI - Gishwati")
plot(ndvi_mukura, main = "NDVI - Mukura")

```

This was used: **MODIS/Terra MOD13Q1 NDVI 16-day composites** for **2020
to 2024**

```{r}
library(terra)
library(dplyr)
library(lubridate)


```

Set up MODIS environment/Change `~/MODIS_ARC` to your preferred folder
path.

```{r}
#shapefile_path <- "/Users/blaise/Rwanda_National Parks/Code_Species_Modeling/shapefiles"

# Load reserve shapefile

```

```{r}
system("gdalinfo --version")

system("which R")


```

```{r}
  PROJ_LIB  = "/opt/homebrew/Caskroom/miniforge/base/envs/gdal_hdf4/share/proj",

)



```

```{r}
Sys.which("R")
# Should return /opt/homebrew/Caskroom/miniforge/base/envs/gdal_hdf4/bin/R

library(terra)
terra::gdal()

```

Read your MODIS HDF4

```{r}
library(terra)

# Define path
#modis_path <- "/Users/blaise/Rwanda_National Parks/Code_Species_Modeling/Gishwati_Mukura/MOD13Q1_061"

# List all HDF files in the folder
hdf_files <- list.files(modis_path, pattern = "\\.hdf$", full.names = TRUE)

# Check number of files
length(hdf_files)


```

```{r}
sds_test <- sds(hdf_files[1])
names(sds_test)


```

Process and crop NDVI Since NDVI is the **first SDS (index `1`)**

Check the actual SDS metadata

```{r}
library(terra)

hdf_file <- hdf_files[1]
info <- terra::sources(sds(hdf_file))
print(info)


```

```{r}
library(terra)

# Test one file
f <- hdf_files[1]
info <- sources(sds(f))
ndvi_path <- info[1]  # First subdataset (NDVI)
cat("Reading NDVI from:\n", ndvi_path, "\n")

ndvi <- rast(ndvi_path)
plot(ndvi)
summary(values(ndvi))

```

```{r}
library(terra)

ndvi_list <- list()

for (f in hdf_files) {
  cat("Processing:", basename(f), "\n")
  
  # Get the actual subdataset paths
  sds_info <- sources(sds(f))
  ndvi_path <- sds_info[1]  # NDVI
  
  # Read raster
  ndvi <- rast(ndvi_path)
  
  # Apply scaling
  ndvi <- ndvi * 0.0001
  
  # Reproject, crop, mask
  ndvi_proj <- project(ndvi, crs(gmb_reserve_vect))
  ndvi_crop <- crop(ndvi_proj, gmb_reserve_vect)
  ndvi_mask <- mask(ndvi_crop, gmb_reserve_vect)
  
  # Store in list
  ndvi_list[[basename(f)]] <- ndvi_mask
}

cat("✅ Processing complete. Files processed:", length(ndvi_list), "\n")

```

Build `ndvi_stack` lazily (on disk): Consider this option

```{r}
library(terra)

# Output file path for the stacked NDVI
stack_path <- "ndvi_stack.tif"

# Check if file exists (to avoid overwriting)
if (!file.exists(stack_path)) {
  # Use lapply to get lazy raster references for each file
  ndvi_layers <- lapply(hdf_files, function(f) {
    sds_info <- sources(sds(f))
    ndvi_path <- sds_info[1]   # First subdataset = NDVI
    rast(ndvi_path) * 0.0001   # Apply scaling factor
  })
  
  # Combine into one stack and write to disk
  ndvi_stack <- rast(ndvi_layers)
  writeRaster(ndvi_stack, stack_path, overwrite = TRUE)
} else {
  # Load existing stack from disk
  ndvi_stack <- rast(stack_path)
}

# Verify metadata
ndvi_stack

```

Convert your list to a single raster stack

```{r}
library(terra)

# Combine all rasters into one SpatRaster (stack)
ndvi_stack <- rast(ndvi_list)

# Verify
ndvi_stack

```

\
Add proper time information

MODIS file names contain the **year and Julian day**. Extract and
convert to actual dates:

```{r}
# Extract dates from filenames
dates <- gsub(".*A|\\.hdf.*", "", basename(hdf_files))
dates <- substr(dates, 1, 7)

# Convert to Date format
dates <- as.Date(dates, format = "%Y%j")

# Assign time to raster stack
time(ndvi_stack) <- dates

# Verify
ndvi_stack
time(ndvi_stack)

```

Plot for a quick check

```{r}
# Plot the first NDVI layer
plot(ndvi_stack[[1]], main = paste("NDVI on", time(ndvi_stack)[1]))

# Quick animation of first few layers
plot(ndvi_stack[[1:5]])

```

##In case you are interested in **Seasonal metrics**

Capture intra-annual seasonality: This is great if invasion success
depends on wet vs dry seasons.

```{r}
# Extract year and month
dates <- time(ndvi_stack)
years <- format(dates, "%Y")
months <- format(dates, "%m")

# Example: mean NDVI per month (across years)
monthly_mean <- tapp(ndvi_stack, months, fun = mean, na.rm = TRUE)
names(monthly_mean) <- month.abb

```

Or Inter-annual trends. Detect productivity changes that may favor
invasions:

Pixels with positive slopes are greening, negative slopes are degrading.

```{r}
library(bfast)

# Compute mean NDVI per year
years <- format(time(ndvi_stack), "%Y")
ndvi_yearly <- tapp(ndvi_stack, years, mean, na.rm = TRUE)

# Fit pixel-wise trend (linear)
ndvi_trend <- app(ndvi_yearly, function(x) {
  if (all(is.na(x))) return(NA)
  lm_fit <- lm(x ~ seq_along(x))
  coef(lm_fit)[2]  # slope
})
plot(ndvi_trend, main = "NDVI Trend (2015–2024)")

```

Check values in our stack

```{r}
global(ndvi_stack, range, na.rm = TRUE)

```

```{r}
# Scale NDVI stack to correct range (-0.2 to +1.0)
ndvi_stack <- ndvi_stack * 0.0001

# Check values again
global(ndvi_stack, range, na.rm = TRUE)


```

**\
Consider this part.** Derive temporal summaries from the NDVI stack

Aggregate NDVI across time to capture stable productivity patterns

```{r}
library(terra)

# Mean NDVI over time
ndvi_mean <- app(ndvi_stack, mean, na.rm = TRUE)

# Standard deviation (variability)
ndvi_sd <- app(ndvi_stack, sd, na.rm = TRUE)

# Min and Max NDVI
ndvi_min <- app(ndvi_stack, min, na.rm = TRUE)
ndvi_max <- app(ndvi_stack, max, na.rm = TRUE)

```

```{r}
# Define path
output_dir <- "/Users/blaise/Rwanda_National Parks/Code_Species_Modeling/Gishwati_Mukura"

# Create directory if it doesn't exist

  dir.create(output_dir, recursive = TRUE)
  cat("✅ Directory created:", output_dir, "\n")
} else {
  cat("ℹ️ Directory already exists:", output_dir, "\n")
}


```

Save your NDVI layers

```{r}

writeRaster(ndvi_sd,   file.path(output_dir, "ndvi_sd.tif"),   overwrite = TRUE)
writeRaster(ndvi_min,  file.path(output_dir, "ndvi_min.tif"),  overwrite = TRUE)
writeRaster(ndvi_max,  file.path(output_dir, "ndvi_max.tif"),  overwrite = TRUE)

cat("✅ NDVI layers saved in:", output_dir, "\n")


```

Save stacked NDVI

Captures **temporal dynamics**:

ndvi_mean → overall productivity

ndvi_sd → vegetation variability (seasonality, phenology)

ndvi_min → stress or dry season

ndvi_max → peak vegetation greenness

```{r}
ndvi_longterm <- c(ndvi_mean, ndvi_sd, ndvi_min, ndvi_max)
names(ndvi_longterm) <- c("ndvi_mean", "ndvi_sd", "ndvi_min", "ndvi_max")

writeRaster(ndvi_longterm, file.path(output_dir, "ndvi_longterm_stack.tif"), overwrite = TRUE)
cat("✅ NDVI stack saved as ndvi_longterm_stack.tif\n")


```

```{r}
# Quick visual check of mean NDVI
plot(ndvi_mean, main = "mean NDVI 2015–2024")


```

In Case ndvi_longterm raster is not in environment

```{r}
ndvi_longterm<-rast("/Users/blaise/Rwanda_National Parks/Code_Species_Modeling/Gishwati_Mukura/ndvi_longterm_stack.tif")
```

Loading the GRACE Soil Moisture Raster

Mean: Long-term average soil moisture availability (key for overall
habitat suitability

Standard Deviation (SD): Variability of soil moisture, reflecting
seasonality or stability of soil water conditions

Minimum: Extreme dry conditions (important for drought sensitivity)

Maximum: Extreme wetness or saturation conditions (e.g., flood
tolerance)

```{r}
library(terra)
library(ncdf4)
# Define the file path (adjust extension if needed, e.g. .nc)
grace_path <- "/Users/blaise/Rwanda_National Parks/Code_Species_Modeling/Gishwati_Mukura/GRACEDADM_CLSM025GL_7D"

# List all HDF files in the folder
grace_files <- list.files(grace_path, pattern = "\\.nc4$", full.names = TRUE)

# Check number of files
length(grace_files)
# Rasterize the first time slice (or list layers)
r <- rast(grace_files)

# View layer names and metadata
print(r)

```

Surface soil moisture percentile

```{r}
# Select all layers with 'sfsm_inst'
sfsm_layers <- r[[grep("sfsm_inst", names(r))]]

# Check the time stamps for these layers
time(sfsm_layers)[1:10]

```

```{r}
library(terra)

# Paths and preparation
# -------------------------------
output_dir <- "/Users/blaise/Rwanda_National Parks/Code_Species_Modeling/Gishwati_Mukura"

if (!dir.exists(output_dir)) {
  dir.create(output_dir, recursive = TRUE)
  cat("✅ Directory created:", output_dir, "\n")
} else {
  cat("ℹ️ Directory already exists:", output_dir, "\n")
}



```

\

```{r}
library(terra)
library(sf)

# Ensure vector is sf object first
#gmb_reserve_vect <- st_read("path_to_gmb_reserve.shp")

# Force to match CRS and axis order of raster
gmb_reserve_vecta <- st_transform(gmb_reserve, crs(sfsm_layers))

# Convert to SpatVector for terra operations
gmb_reserve_vecta <- vect(gmb_reserve_vecta)


```

```{r}
library(terra)


# Get extent of your vector
v_ext <- ext(gmb_reserve_vecta)

# Create a new raster with desired resolution over the vector extent
highres_raster_template <- rast(
  xmin = v_ext[1],
  xmax = v_ext[2],
  ymin = v_ext[3],
  ymax = v_ext[4],
  resolution = 0.01,       # higher resolution
  crs = crs(sfsm_layers)   # same CRS as original raster
)

# Resample original raster onto the high-res template
sfsm_layers_highres <- resample(sfsm_layers, highres_raster_template)

# Crop to vector
clipped_highres <- crop(sfsm_layers_highres, gmb_reserve_vecta)

# Plot with vector overlay
plot(clipped_highres)
plot(gmb_reserve_vecta, add = TRUE, border = "red", lwd = 2)


```

```{r}
library(terra)

# -------------------------------
# Mask clipped raster to the reserve
# -------------------------------
masked_raster <- mask(clipped_highres, gmb_reserve_vecta)

# -------------------------------
# Compute temporal statistics
# -------------------------------
sfsm_mean  <- app(masked_raster, mean, na.rm = TRUE)
sfsm_sd    <- app(masked_raster, sd, na.rm = TRUE)
sfsm_min   <- app(masked_raster, min, na.rm = TRUE)
sfsm_max   <- app(masked_raster, max, na.rm = TRUE)

# -------------------------------
# Save outputs
# -------------------------------
writeRaster(sfsm_mean, file.path(output_dir, "sfsm_mean.tif"), overwrite = TRUE)
writeRaster(sfsm_sd,   file.path(output_dir, "sfsm_sd.tif"),   overwrite = TRUE)
writeRaster(sfsm_min,  file.path(output_dir, "sfsm_min.tif"),  overwrite = TRUE)
writeRaster(sfsm_max,  file.path(output_dir, "sfsm_max.tif"),  overwrite = TRUE)

# -------------------------------
# Stack for modeling
# -------------------------------
sfsm_longterm <- c(sfsm_mean, sfsm_sd, sfsm_min, sfsm_max)
names(sfsm_longterm) <- c("sfsm_mean", "sfsm_sd", "sfsm_min", "sfsm_max")

writeRaster(sfsm_longterm,
            file.path(output_dir, "sfsm_longterm.tif"),
            overwrite = TRUE)


```

```{r}

# -------------------------------
# Optional: plot result with vector overlay
# -------------------------------
plot(sfsm_longterm)
plot(gmb_reserve_vecta, add = TRUE, border = "red", lwd = 2)

```

\
Prepare Presence Data for Acacia Species

```{r}
# Example:  Only Presence points for each species as CSV with lat/lon
#acacia_mearnsii <- read.csv("acacia_mearnsii_points.csv")  # must contain lon, lat
#acacia_melanoxylon <- read.csv("acacia_melanoxylon_points.csv")

#alnus_acuminata<-read.csv("alnus_acuminata_points.csv")
# Convert sf to SpatVector
am_pts <- vect(acacia_mearnsii_points)      # Acacia mearnsii presence points
ax_pts <- vect(acacia_melanoxylon_points)   # Acacia melanoxylon presence points
al_pts<-vect(alnus_acuminata_points).   # Alnus acuminata presence

```

```{r}
# Load libraries
library(terra)
library(raster)
library(dismo)

# Optional: set path to MaxEnt .jar if needed
# dismo::maxent() requires that you download maxent.jar and place it in the correct location
# download.file("https://biodiversityinformatics.amnh.org/open_source/maxent/maxent.php?op=download", "maxent.zip")
# unzip("maxent.zip")
# Place maxent.jar in a known directory and set the path:
# maxent_path <- "path/to/maxent.jar"
# options(dismo.maxent = maxent_path)

# Convert predictors (SpatRaster) to RasterStack
predictors_raster <- raster::stack(predictors)


```

Run MaxEnt Model (Presence-only)\
Mask invalid areas (ensure complete overlap of rasters)

```{r}
# Create a unified mask (non-NA in all predictors)
valid_mask <- !is.na(sum(predictors))

# Apply the mask to limit the study area
predictors <- mask(predictors, valid_mask)

```

Filter presence points to remove those on NA cells

```{r}
# Convert predictors to raster for compatibility
predictors_raster <- raster::stack(predictors)

# --- Acacia mearnsii ---
# Convert SpatVector to coordinates
am_coords <- crds(am_pts)

# Remove presence points with NA predictor values
am_coords_clean <- am_coords[!is.na(extract(predictors_raster, am_coords)[,1]), ]


```

Run MaxEnt model with cleaned data

Update workflow and Predictors formation\

```{r}
library(terra)
library(dismo)
library(raster)  # For compatibility with dismo


# Create a unified mask of valid data (non-NA)
valid_mask <- app(predictors, fun = function(...) all(!is.na(c(...))))

# Apply the mask to all predictors
predictors <- mask(predictors, valid_mask)

# Convert to raster stack for dismo::maxent
predictors_raster <- stack(predictors)

# ----------------------------------------------------------------
# Function to clean points and run MaxEnt
run_maxent <- function(pres_pts, predictors_raster) {
  # Extract coordinates
  coords <- terra::crds(pres_pts)
  
  # Remove points on NA areas
  vals <- raster::extract(predictors_raster, coords)
  clean_coords <- coords[complete.cases(vals), ]
  
  # Define background sampling area (valid mask)
  bg <- dismo::randomPoints(predictors_raster[[1]], n = 10000, ext = raster::extent(predictors_raster),
                            excludep = TRUE, warn = 1)
  
  # Final check: Remove any background points with NA values
  bg_vals <- raster::extract(predictors_raster, bg)
  bg_clean <- bg[complete.cases(bg_vals), ]
  
  # Fit MaxEnt model
  model <- dismo::maxent(x = predictors_raster, p = clean_coords, a = bg_clean)
  
  # Predict suitability
  prediction <- predict(predictors_raster, model)
  
  return(list(model = model, prediction = prediction))
}


```

```{r}
# ----------------------------------------------------------------
# Run for Acacia mearnsii
am_result <- run_maxent(am_pts, predictors_raster)
plot(am_result$prediction, main = "Habitat Suitability - Acacia mearnsii",xlab = "Longitude",
  ylab = "Latitude",
  axes = TRUE)


```

Update

```{r}
  main = "Habitat Suitability - Acacia mearnsii",
```

Plot in raster format\

```{r}
library(terra)
library(ggplot2)
library(sf)
library(viridis)


# Convert RasterLayer to SpatRaster if needed
ax_pred_raster <- rast(ax_result$prediction)

# Reproject to WGS84
ax_pred_raster <- project(ax_pred_raster, "EPSG:4326")

# Convert to data frame
ax_df <- as.data.frame(ax_pred_raster, xy = TRUE, na.rm = TRUE)
names(ax_df)[3] <- "suitability"

# Ensure vector is sf and reprojected
if (!inherits(gmb_reserve_vect, "sf")) {
  gmb_reserve_vect <- st_as_sf(gmb_reserve_vect)
}
gmb_reserve_vect <- st_transform(gmb_reserve_vect, crs = 4326)

# Plot
ggplot(ax_df, aes(x = x, y = y, fill = suitability)) +
  geom_tile() +
  scale_fill_viridis(
    name = "Suitability",
    option = "viridis",
    direction = -1
  ) +
  geom_sf(data = gmb_reserve_vect, inherit.aes = FALSE, fill = NA, color = "blue", linewidth = 1) +
  labs(
    title = "Smoothed Habitat Suitability - Acacia melanoxylon",
    x = "Longitude",
    y = "Latitude"
  ) +
  coord_sf() +
  theme_minimal()

```

\
This also work :Due to error of factor that turn to numeric

```{r}
# Create a better valid area mask (avoid coercing factors)
# Identify non-factor layers
non_factor_names <- names(predictors)[!sapply(predictors, is.factor)]

# Create a SpatRaster with only non-factor layers
predictor_valid <- predictors[[non_factor_names]]

# Create mask without factors
valid_mask <- app(predictor_valid, fun = function(...) all(!is.na(c(...))))

# Apply it to the full predictor set (including factors)
predictors_masked <- mask(predictors, valid_mask)


# Raster stack conversion for dismo compatibility
predictors_raster <- raster::stack(predictors)

```

```{r}

library(rJava)

```

Instead of using the full extent, **use the valid mask directly**:

```{r}
run_maxent <- function(pres_pts, predictors_raster) {
  coords <- terra::crds(pres_pts)
  vals <- raster::extract(predictors_raster, coords)
  clean_coords <- coords[complete.cases(vals), ]

  # Better background points sampling using valid mask
  bg_mask <- !is.na(predictors_raster[[1]])
  bg_points <- dismo::randomPoints(bg_mask, n = 10000, excludep = TRUE)

  # Final background cleanup
  bg_vals <- raster::extract(predictors_raster, bg_points)
  bg_clean <- bg_points[complete.cases(bg_vals), ]

  # MaxEnt model
  model <- dismo::maxent(x = predictors_raster, p = clean_coords, a = bg_clean)
  prediction <- predict(predictors_raster, model)

  return(list(model = model, prediction = prediction))
}

```

```{r}
# Run models Acacia mearnsii
am_result <- run_maxent(am_pts, predictors_raster)
plot(am_result$prediction, main = "Habitat Suitability - Acacia mearnsii")


```

```{r}
#Acacia melanoxylon
ax_result <- run_maxent(ax_pts, predictors_raster)
plot(ax_result$prediction, main = "Habitat Suitability - Acacia melanoxylon")
```

Export Soil and Acacia mearnsii suitability\

```{r}
library(tmap)
library(terra)
library(sf)

# Convert to SpatRaster if needed
am_pred_raster <- rast(am_result$prediction)

# Reproject data to EPSG:4326
soil_crop_rs <- terra::project(soil_crop_rs, "EPSG:4326")
am_pred_raster <- terra::project(am_pred_raster, "EPSG:4326")
gmb_reserve_vect <- st_transform(st_as_sf(gmb_reserve_vect), crs = 4326)

# Define the tmap plot
map_plot <- tm_shape(soil_crop_rs) +
  tm_raster(
    col.scale = tm_scale_categorical(),
    col.legend = tm_legend(title = "Soil Type")
  ) +
  tm_shape(am_pred_raster) +
  tm_raster(
    col.scale = tm_scale(values = "viridis", reverse = TRUE),
    col_alpha = 0.6,
    col.legend = tm_legend(title = "A. mearnsii Suitability")
  ) +
  tm_shape(gmb_reserve_vect) +
  tm_borders(col = "blue", lwd = 2) +
  tm_title("A. mearnsii vs Soil Type") +
  tm_layout(frame = TRUE) +
  tm_xlab("Longitude") +
  tm_ylab("Latitude") +
  tm_compass(position = c("right", "top"))

# Save as PNG
tmap_save(map_plot, filename = "Acacia mearnsii_suitability_soil.png", width = 8, height = 6, dpi = 600)

# Save as SVG
tmap_save(map_plot, filename = "Acacia mearnsii_suitability_soil.svg", width = 8, height = 6)
```

```{r}

if (!inherits(gmb_reserve_vect, "sf")) {
  gmb_reserve_vect <- st_as_sf(gmb_reserve_vect)
}
gmb_reserve_vect <- st_transform(gmb_reserve_vect, crs = 4326)

# Define bounding box manually (optional)
bbox_custom <- st_bbox(c(xmin = 29.1, xmax = 29.8, ymin = -2.10, ymax = -1.70), crs = st_crs(4326))

# Set tmap to plot mode
tmap_mode("plot")

# Final plot with v4 syntax
tm_shape(soil_crop_rs, bbox = bbox_custom) +
  tm_raster(
    col.scale = tm_scale_categorical(),
```
