```{r}
  filter(Algorithm == "ENSEMBLE")

# Create heatmap plot consistent with previous style
ensemble_plot <- ggplot(
  ensemble_varimp,
  aes(
    x = Species,
    y = reorder(term, importance),  # order by variable importance
    fill = importance
  )
) +
  geom_tile() +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(name = "Ensemble Importance") +
  labs(
    title = "Environmental Variable Importance (Ensemble Models, Gishwati)",
    x = "Species",
    y = "Environmental Variable"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1, color = "black"),
    axis.text.y = element_text(color = "black"),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    legend.position = "right"
  )

# Display the plot
print(ensemble_plot)
```

Export metrics

```{r}

# Load libraries
library(terra)
library(dplyr)
library(readr)
library(purrr)
library(tibble)

# Define export folder
outdir <- "/Users/blaise/Rwanda_National Parks/Code_Species_Modeling/Ensemble_Gishwati"
if (!dir.exists(outdir)) dir.create(outdir, recursive = TRUE)

# --- Loop over species in results_gishwati ---
for (sp in names(results_gishwati$results)) {
  cat("\n==== Processing species:", sp, "====\n")
  
  res <- results_gishwati$results[[sp]]
  if (is.null(res)) {
    cat("Species result is NULL, skipping...\n")
    next
  }
  
 
  # -----------------------
  # 2. METRICS + CV SUMMARY
  # -----------------------
  all_metrics <- res$metrics %>% mutate(Species = sp, Site = results_gishwati$site)
  
  cv_summary <- purrr::imap_dfr(res$cv_predictions, ~{
    tibble::tibble(
      Algorithm = .y,
      min_pred  = min(.x, na.rm = TRUE),
      mean_pred = mean(.x, na.rm = TRUE),
      max_pred  = max(.x, na.rm = TRUE),
      Species   = sp,
      Site      = results_gishwati$site
    )
  })
  
  final_table <- left_join(all_metrics, cv_summary, by = c("Algorithm", "Species", "Site"))
  
  # Export metrics + CV summary
  readr::write_csv(final_table,
    file.path(outdir, paste0(sp, "_metrics_with_cv_summary.csv")))
  
  # -----------------------
  # 3. RAW CV PREDICTIONS
  # -----------------------
  cv_tbl <- purrr::imap_dfr(res$cv_predictions, ~{
    tibble::tibble(
      Algorithm = .y,
      Fold_ID   = seq_along(.x),
      Predicted = .x,
      Species   = sp,
      Site      = results_gishwati$site
    )
  })
  readr::write_csv(cv_tbl,
    file.path(outdir, paste0(sp, "_cv_predictions_raw.csv")))
  
  # -----------------------
  # 4. ENSEMBLE WEIGHTS
  # -----------------------
  weights_df <- tibble::enframe(res$weights, name = "model", value = "weight")
  readr::write_csv(weights_df,
    file.path(outdir, paste0(sp, "_weights.csv")))
  
  # -----------------------
  # 5. SAVE SPECIES RESULT OBJECT
  # -----------------------
  saveRDS(res, file.path(outdir, paste0(sp, "_result_object.rds")))
}

# --- Save entire Gishwati object (all species together) ---
saveRDS(results_gishwati, file.path(outdir, "results_gishwati_all_species.rds"))


```

```{r}
library(pROC)

# --- 1. Define output directory ---
outdir <- "/Users/blaise/Rwanda_National Parks/Code_Species_Modeling/Ensemble_Gishwati"
if (!dir.exists(outdir)) dir.create(outdir, recursive = TRUE)

# --- 2. Define colors for models ---
cols <- c(BRT = "gray", RF = "lightblue", MAXENT = "lightgreen", ENSEMBLE = "brown")

# --- 3. Loop through all species ---
for (sp in names(results_gishwati$results)) {
  
  sp_res <- results_gishwati$results[[sp]]
  
  # skip species if predictions missing
  if (is.null(sp_res$cv_predictions)) next
  
  obs <- sp_res$data_used$presence
  
  # compute ROC for each algorithm safely
  roc_list <- list()
  for (m in names(cols)) {
    if (!is.null(sp_res$cv_predictions[[m]])) {
      roc_list[[m]] <- roc(obs, sp_res$cv_predictions[[m]], quiet = TRUE)
    }
  }
  
  # if no ROC available, skip
  if (length(roc_list) == 0) next
  
  # --- 4. Save each species ROC to PDF ---
  pdf(file.path(outdir, paste0("ROC_", sp, ".pdf")),
      width = 7, height = 7, useDingbats = FALSE)
  
  first <- TRUE
  for (m in names(roc_list)) {
    if (first) {
      plot(roc_list[[m]], col = cols[m], lwd = 2, 
           main = paste("ROC Curves -", sp))
      first <- FALSE
    } else {
      plot(roc_list[[m]], col = cols[m], lwd = 2, add = TRUE)
    }
  }
  
  legend("bottomright",
         legend = sapply(names(roc_list), function(m) {
           paste0(m, " (AUC=", round(auc(roc_list[[m]]), 3), ")")
         }),
         col = cols[names(roc_list)], lwd = 2, bty = "n")
  
  dev.off()
}

cat("✅ ROC curves exported to:", outdir, "\n")

```

Forest at risk

```{r}
compute_forest_risk_gradient <- function(sp_name, species_result, forest_mask, outdir) {
  message("=== compute_forest_risk: ", sp_name, " ===")
  
  # --- Basic checks
  if (is.null(species_result) || !is.list(species_result))
    stop("species_result must be a non-null list (model result).")
  if (is.null(species_result$rasters$ENSEMBLE))
    stop("No ensemble raster found in species_result$rasters$ENSEMBLE")
  
  ens_rast <- species_result$rasters$ENSEMBLE
  if (!inherits(ens_rast, "SpatRaster")) stop("ENSEMBLE raster is not a SpatRaster")
  if (terra::nlyr(ens_rast) > 1) ens_rast <- ens_rast[[1]]

  # --- Align forest mask
  if (!terra::compareGeom(ens_rast, forest_mask, stopOnError = FALSE)) {
    message("  - Aligning/resampling forest_mask to ensemble raster resolution/extent (nearest)")
    forest_mask2 <- terra::resample(forest_mask, ens_rast, method = "near")
  } else {
    forest_mask2 <- forest_mask
  }
  forest_mask2 <- terra::ifel(forest_mask2 > 0.5, 1, 0)

  # --- Compute threshold (Youden)
  ens_pred <- as.numeric(species_result$cv_predictions$ENSEMBLE)
  y <- species_result$data_used$presence
  roc_obj <- tryCatch(pROC::roc(y, ens_pred, quiet = TRUE), error = function(e) NULL)
  thr <- if (is.null(roc_obj)) 0.5 else
    as.numeric(pROC::coords(roc_obj, "best", ret = "threshold", best.method = "youden")[1])
  message(sprintf("  - threshold (Youden / fallback) = %.4f", thr))

  # --- Mask forest only, keep ensemble gradient
  risk_gradient <- terra::ifel(forest_mask2 == 1, ens_rast, NA)

  # --- Compute forest and risk areas
  g_f <- terra::global(forest_mask2, "sum", na.rm = TRUE)
  n_forest <- as.numeric(g_f[1])
  pixel_area_km2 <- 0.0004  # for 20m × 20m
  forest_area_km2 <- n_forest * pixel_area_km2

  binary_risk <- terra::ifel(risk_gradient > thr, 1, 0)
  g_r <- terra::global(binary_risk, "sum", na.rm = TRUE)
  n_risk <- as.numeric(g_r[1])
  risk_area_km2 <- n_risk * pixel_area_km2
  pct_risk <- 100 * (risk_area_km2 / forest_area_km2)

  message(sprintf("  - Forest area: %.2f km²; Risk area: %.2f km² (%.2f%%)",
                  forest_area_km2, risk_area_km2, pct_risk))

 
# --- Plot gradient map with non-forest subdued
pdf(file.path(outdir, paste0(sp_name, "_forest_risk_gradient.pdf")),
    width = 8, height = 6, useDingbats = FALSE)

# Extract non-NA forest values for scaling
vals <- terra::values(risk_gradient, na.rm = TRUE)
low <- min(vals, na.rm = TRUE)
high <- max(vals, na.rm = TRUE)

# Plot the gradient over forest
plot(risk_gradient,
     main = paste0(sp_name, " – Gradient Forest Invasion Risk"),
     col = colorRampPalette(c("forestgreen", "yellow", "brown"))(100),
     range = c(low, high),
     axes = TRUE)

# Overlay non-forest pixels with transparency
non_forest <- forest_mask2 == 0
plot(non_forest, add = TRUE,
     col = adjustcolor("forestgreen", alpha.f = 0.2),
     legend = FALSE)

# --- Add legend *on top* of overlays ---
legend("right",
       inset = 0.02,
       legend = c("Low risk", "High risk", "Non-forest"),
       fill = c("forestgreen", "brown", adjustcolor("forestgreen", alpha.f = 0.3)),
       border = "grey30",
       bty = "o",           # box drawn
       title = "Forest Invasion Risk",
       bg = adjustcolor("white", alpha.f = 0.8))  # visible white background



dev.off()
message("  - Gradient risk map PDF saved (legend overlay corrected)")


  # --- Return both summary and raster
  out_df <- data.frame(
    Species = sp_name,
    Threshold = as.numeric(thr),
    Forest_area = as.numeric(forest_area_km2),
    Forest_risk_area = as.numeric(risk_area_km2),
    Percent_risk = as.numeric(pct_risk),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )

  return(list(summary = out_df, raster = risk_gradient))
}


```

MUKURA

CODE WITH CROSS BLOCK AND WEIGHT_SCHEME

```{r}
# Mukura (filter inside wrapper: only Acacia_mearnsii will remain)
result_mukura <- run_sdm_species_site(
  site_name = "Mukura",
  species_name = "Acacia_mearnsii",
  presence_xy = acacia_mearnsii_points_mukura,
  background_xy = background_mukura_xy_clean,
  predictors_stack = mukura_stack_raster,
cv_type          = "spatial",
  weight_schemes   = candidate_weights,   # <-- passed once for all species
  )
```

```{r}
str(result_mukura, 1)

```

```{r}
# 1) Metrics for individual algorithms
cat("\n--- Individual model metrics ---\n")
print(result_mukura$metrics)

```

```{r}
# ---- Combine weights + metrics into one tidy table (single species) ----
combine_weights_metrics_single <- function(result, site_name, species_name) {
  if (is.null(result)) return(NULL)
  
  # extract weights (BRT, RF, MAXENT per scheme)
  w_df <- dplyr::bind_rows(lapply(names(result$weights_schemes), function(sch) {
    w <- result$weights_schemes[[sch]]
    tibble::tibble(
      Site = site_name,
      Species = species_name,
      Scheme = sch,
      Weight_BRT = unname(w["BRT"]),
      Weight_RF = unname(w["RF"]),
      Weight_MAXENT = unname(w["MAXENT"])
    )
  }))
  
  # extract metrics (AUC, AUC_PR, TSS per scheme)
  m_df <- dplyr::bind_rows(lapply(names(result$ensemble_schemes), function(sch) {
    m <- result$ensemble_schemes[[sch]]$metrics
    tibble::tibble(
      Site = site_name,
      Species = species_name,
      Scheme = sch,
      AUC = m$AUC,
      AUC_PR = m$AUC_PR,
      TSS = m$TSS
    )
  }))
  
  # join weights + metrics by Scheme
  dplyr::left_join(w_df, m_df, by = c("Site", "Species", "Scheme"))
}

# ---- Example usage for Mukura ----
weights_metrics_mukura <- combine_weights_metrics_single(result_mukura, 
                                                         site_name = "Mukura",
                                                         species_name = "Acacia_mearnsii")

# Inspect
print(weights_metrics_mukura)

```

```{r}
# === Extract Moran’s I summary for Mukura site ===
outdir <- "/Users/blaise/Rwanda_National Parks/Code_Species_Modeling/Ensemble_Mukura"
if (!dir.exists(outdir)) dir.create(outdir, recursive = TRUE)

# Convert list of Moran test results into a clean data frame
resid_summary_table <- do.call(rbind, lapply(names(result_mukura$moran_test), function(model_name) {
  x <- result_mukura$moran_test[[model_name]]
  data.frame(
    Species     = "Acacia_mearnsii",  # add species name manually
    Model       = model_name,
    Moran_I     = round(as.numeric(x$statistic), 3),
    Expectation = round(as.numeric(x$expectation), 3),
    Variance    = round(as.numeric(x$variance), 3),
    SD          = round(as.numeric(x$sd), 3),
    P_value     = signif(as.numeric(x$p.value), 3)
  )
}))

# Save as CSV
write.csv(
  resid_summary_table,
  file = file.path(outdir, "moran_summary_mukura.csv"),
  row.names = FALSE
)

# Display in console
print(resid_summary_table)

```

Rasters

```{r}

library(terra)

# Output directory
outdir <- "/Users/blaise/Rwanda_National Parks/Code_Species_Modeling/Ensemble_Mukura"
if (!dir.exists(outdir)) dir.create(outdir, recursive = TRUE)

# Loop over rasters
for (alg in names(result_mukura$rasters)) {
  r <- result_mukura$rasters[[alg]]
  
  cat("\n==== Algorithm:", alg, "====\n")
  
  if (is.null(r)) {
    cat("Raster is NULL\n")
    next
  }
  
  # Print summary
  print(terra::summary(r))
  
  # Print raster structure
  print(r)
  
  # Min/max values
  print(terra::minmax(r))
  
  # Plot raster
  plot(r, main = paste("Mukura -", alg))
  
  # Export raster
  fname <- file.path(outdir, paste0("Mukura_", alg, "_suitability.tif"))
  tryCatch({
    terra::writeRaster(r, fname, overwrite = TRUE)
    cat("Exported raster to:", fname, "\n")
  }, error = function(e) {
    warning(sprintf("Failed to write %s: %s", fname, e$message))
  })
}

```

```{r}
library(terra)

# --- Output directory ---
outdir <- "/Users/blaise/Rwanda_National Parks/Code_Species_Modeling/Ensemble_Mukura_Figures"
if (!dir.exists(outdir)) dir.create(outdir, recursive = TRUE)

# --- Export rasters as high-res PDFs ---
r_list <- result_mukura$rasters

# Names for each raster (fallback if not named)
r_names <- if (!is.null(names(r_list)) && all(nzchar(names(r_list)))) {
  names(r_list)
} else {
  paste0("raster_", seq_along(r_list))
}

# Loop and export
for (i in seq_along(r_list)) {
  r <- r_list[[i]]
  fname <- file.path(outdir, paste0(r_names[i], ".pdf"))
  
  pdf(fname, width = 8, height = 7, pointsize = 10)   # size in inches
  plot(r, main = paste("Raster:", r_names[i]))
  dev.off()
}

message("✅ Export complete: ", length(r_list), " rasters saved as PDFs in ", outdir)
```


Ensemble metrics OUT

```{r}
library(dplyr)

library(readr)
library(purrr)

# Define output directory
outdir <- "/Users/blaise/Rwanda_National Parks/Code_Species_Modeling/Ensemble_Mukura"
if (!dir.exists(outdir)) dir.create(outdir, recursive = TRUE)

# --- 1. Metrics table (already contains BRT, RF, MAXENT, ENSEMBLE) ---
all_metrics <- result_mukura$metrics %>%
  mutate(Site = "Mukura")

# --- 2. CV predictions summary (min, mean, max per algorithm) ---
cv_summary <- purrr::imap_dfr(result_mukura$cv_predictions, ~{
  tibble::tibble(
    Algorithm = .y,
    min_pred  = min(.x, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_pred = mean(.x, na.rm = TRUE),
    max_pred  = max(.x, na.rm = TRUE)
  )
})

# --- 3. Merge metrics + CV summaries ---
final_table <- left_join(all_metrics, cv_summary, by = "Algorithm")

# --- 4. Save both metrics and CV details ---
# (a) full metrics + CV summary
readr::write_csv(final_table,
  file.path(outdir, "Mukura_metrics_with_cv_summary.csv"))

# (b) raw CV predictions (long format, fold-level)
cv_tbl <- purrr::imap_dfr(result_mukura$cv_predictions, ~{
  tibble::tibble(
    Algorithm = .y,
    Fold_ID   = seq_along(.x),
    Predicted = .x,
    Site      = "Mukura"
  )
})
readr::write_csv(cv_tbl,
  file.path(outdir, "Mukura_cv_predictions_raw.csv"))

# --- Check output ---
print(final_table)

```

Plot of ROC

```{r}

library(pROC)

# --- 1. True labels ---
obs <- result_mukura$data_used$presence

# --- 2. Compute ROC for each algorithm ---
roc_list <- list(
  BRT      = roc(obs, result_mukura$cv_predictions$BRT, quiet = TRUE),
  RF       = roc(obs, result_mukura$cv_predictions$RF, quiet = TRUE),
  MAXENT   = roc(obs, result_mukura$cv_predictions$MAXENT, quiet = TRUE),
  ENSEMBLE = roc(obs, result_mukura$cv_predictions$ENSEMBLE, quiet = TRUE)
)

# --- 3. Colors for plotting ---
cols <- c(BRT = "gray", RF = "lightblue", MAXENT = "lightgreen", ENSEMBLE = "brown")

# --- 4. Plot all ROC curves ---
plot(roc_list$BRT, col = cols["BRT"], lwd = 2, main = "ROC Curves for Mukura Models")
plot(roc_list$RF, col = cols["RF"], lwd = 2, add = TRUE)
plot(roc_list$MAXENT, col = cols["MAXENT"], lwd = 2, add = TRUE)
plot(roc_list$ENSEMBLE, col = cols["ENSEMBLE"], lwd = 2, add = TRUE)

# --- 5. Add legend with AUC values ---
legend("bottomright",
       legend = sapply(names(roc_list), function(m) {
         paste0(m, " (AUC=", round(auc(roc_list[[m]]), 3), ")")
       }),
       col = cols, lwd = 2, bty = "n")

```

```{r}
library(pROC)

# --- 1. Define output directory ---
outdir <- "/Users/blaise/Rwanda_National Parks/Code_Species_Modeling/Ensemble_Mukura"
if (!dir.exists(outdir)) dir.create(outdir, recursive = TRUE)

# --- 2. True labels ---
obs <- result_mukura$data_used$presence

# --- 3. Compute ROC for each algorithm ---
roc_list <- list(
  BRT      = roc(obs, result_mukura$cv_predictions$BRT, quiet = TRUE),
  RF       = roc(obs, result_mukura$cv_predictions$RF, quiet = TRUE),
  MAXENT   = roc(obs, result_mukura$cv_predictions$MAXENT, quiet = TRUE),
  ENSEMBLE = roc(obs, result_mukura$cv_predictions$ENSEMBLE, quiet = TRUE)
)

# --- 4. Colors for plotting ---
cols <- c(BRT = "gray", RF = "lightblue", MAXENT = "lightgreen", ENSEMBLE = "brown")

# --- 5. Export to PDF (high resolution, vector format) ---
pdf(file.path(outdir, "ROC_Curves_Mukura.pdf"),
    width = 7, height = 7, useDingbats = FALSE)

plot(roc_list$BRT, col = cols["BRT"], lwd = 2, main = "ROC Curves for Mukura Models")
plot(roc_list$RF, col = cols["RF"], lwd = 2, add = TRUE)
plot(roc_list$MAXENT, col = cols["MAXENT"], lwd = 2, add = TRUE)
plot(roc_list$ENSEMBLE, col = cols["ENSEMBLE"], lwd = 2, add = TRUE)

legend("bottomright",
       legend = sapply(names(roc_list), function(m) {
         paste0(m, " (AUC=", round(auc(roc_list[[m]]), 3), ")")
       }),
       col = cols, lwd = 2, bty = "n")

dev.off()
```

```{r}
  })

# Reorder columns for clarity

# --- Define output path ---
```

Ensemble Variable Importance (Mukura)

```{r}
# --- 1. Libraries ---
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)

# --- 2. Output directory ---
outdir_fig <- "/Users/blaise/Rwanda_National Parks/Code_Species_Modeling/Ensemble_Mukura_Figures"
if (!dir.exists(outdir_fig)) dir.create(outdir_fig, recursive = TRUE)

# --- 3. Extract Ensemble variable importance ---
ensemble_varimp_mukura <- result_mukura$varimp$ENSEMBLE %>%
  dplyr::mutate(Species = "Acacia_mearnsii", Algorithm = "ENSEMBLE")

# --- 4. Plot heatmap ---
ensemble_plot_mukura <- ggplot(
  ensemble_varimp_mukura,
  aes(
    x = Species,
    y = reorder(term, importance),
    fill = importance
  )
) +
  geom_tile() +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(
    name = "Ensemble Importance") +
  labs(
    title = "Environmental Variable Importance – Ensemble Model (Mukura)",
    x = "Species",
    y = "Environmental Variable"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1, color = "black"),
    axis.text.y = element_text(color = "black"),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    legend.position = "right",
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5)
  )

# --- 5. Display in R session ---
print(ensemble_plot_mukura)

# --- 6. Export high-resolution PDF ---
ggsave(
  filename = file.path(outdir_fig, "Heatmap_Ensemble_VarImportance_Mukura.pdf"),
  plot = ensemble_plot_mukura,
  width = 8,
  height = 6,
  dpi = 600,
  useDingbats = FALSE
)

message("✅ Ensemble variable importance heatmap exported successfully for Mukura.")
```

```{r}
# === Set up output directory ===
outdir <- "/Users/blaise/Rwanda_National Parks/Code_Species_Modeling/Ensemble_Mukura_Figures"
if (!dir.exists(outdir)) dir.create(outdir, recursive = TRUE)

# === Create forest mask from NDVI ===
forest_mask <- classify(
  ndvi, 
  rbind(c(-Inf, 0.6, 0),   # NDVI ≤ 0.6 → non-forest
        c(0.6, Inf, 1))    # NDVI > 0.6 → forest
)

# === Define output file path ===
pdf_file <- file.path(outdir, "Forest_mask_Mukura_highres.pdf")

# === Open high-resolution PDF device ===
pdf(
  pdf_file,
  width = 10,          # large format for better detail
  height = 8,
  useDingbats = FALSE
)

# === Plot forest mask ===
plot(
  forest_mask,
  main = "Forest Mask from NDVI – Mukura Landscape",
  col = c("#220135", "forestgreen"),
  legend = FALSE,
  axes = TRUE,
  box = TRUE
)

legend(
  "bottomright",
  legend = c("Non-forest (NDVI < 0.6)", "Forest (NDVI > 0.6)"),
  fill = c("#220135", "forestgreen"),
  border = "grey30",
  bty = "o",
  bg = adjustcolor("white", alpha.f = 0.8),
  title = "NDVI-based Classification"
)

# === Close PDF device ===
dev.off()

# === Confirmation message ===
message("✅ High-resolution forest mask saved: ", pdf_file)

```

```{r}
# ==============================================================
# Forest invasion risk mapping – Mukura site
# Species: Acacia mearnsii
# ==============================================================
compute_forest_risk_gradient_mukura <- function(sp_name, species_result, forest_mask, outdir) {
  message("=== compute_forest_risk (Mukura): ", sp_name, " ===")

  # --- Basic checks
  if (is.null(species_result) || !is.list(species_result))
    stop("species_result must be a non-null list (model result).")
  if (is.null(species_result$rasters$ENSEMBLE))
    stop("No ensemble raster found in species_result$rasters$ENSEMBLE")

  # --- Get ensemble raster
  ens_rast <- species_result$rasters$ENSEMBLE
  if (is.list(ens_rast)) ens_rast <- ens_rast[[1]]
  if (inherits(ens_rast, "SpatRaster") && terra::nlyr(ens_rast) > 1)
    ens_rast <- ens_rast[[1]]
  if (!inherits(ens_rast, "SpatRaster"))
    stop("ENSEMBLE raster must be a SpatRaster after extraction.")

  # --- Align forest mask
  if (!terra::compareGeom(ens_rast, forest_mask, stopOnError = FALSE)) {
    message("  - Aligning/resampling forest_mask to ensemble raster resolution/extent (nearest)")
    forest_mask2 <- terra::resample(forest_mask, ens_rast, method = "near")
  } else {
    forest_mask2 <- forest_mask
  }
  forest_mask2 <- terra::ifel(forest_mask2 > 0.5, 1, 0)

  # --- Compute Youden (SSS) threshold
  ens_pred <- as.numeric(species_result$cv_predictions$ENSEMBLE)
  y <- species_result$data_used$presence
  roc_obj <- tryCatch(pROC::roc(y, ens_pred, quiet = TRUE), error = function(e) NULL)
  thr <- if (is.null(roc_obj)) 0.5 else
    as.numeric(pROC::coords(roc_obj, "best", ret = "threshold", best.method = "youden")[1])
  message(sprintf("  - threshold (Youden / fallback) = %.4f", thr))

  # --- Mask forest only, keep continuous risk gradient
  risk_gradient <- terra::ifel(forest_mask2 == 1, ens_rast, NA)

  # --- Compute forest and invasion risk area
  g_f <- terra::global(forest_mask2, "sum", na.rm = TRUE)
  n_forest <- as.numeric(g_f[1])
  pixel_area_km2 <- 0.0004  # for 20m × 20m pixel
  forest_area_km2 <- n_forest * pixel_area_km2

  binary_risk <- terra::ifel(risk_gradient > thr, 1, 0)
  g_r <- terra::global(binary_risk, "sum", na.rm = TRUE)
  n_risk <- as.numeric(g_r[1])
  risk_area_km2 <- n_risk * pixel_area_km2
  pct_risk <- 100 * (risk_area_km2 / forest_area_km2)

  message(sprintf("  - Forest area: %.2f km²; Risk area: %.2f km² (%.2f%%)",
                  forest_area_km2, risk_area_km2, pct_risk))

  # --- Plot continuous gradient map ---
  pdf(file.path(outdir, paste0(sp_name, "_forest_risk_gradient_Mukura.pdf")),
      width = 8, height = 6, useDingbats = FALSE)

  vals <- terra::values(risk_gradient, na.rm = TRUE)
  low <- min(vals, na.rm = TRUE)
  high <- max(vals, na.rm = TRUE)

  plot(risk_gradient,
       main = paste0(sp_name, " – Gradient Forest Invasion Risk (Mukura)"),
       col = colorRampPalette(c("forestgreen", "yellow", "brown"))(100),
       range = c(low, high),
       axes = TRUE)

  non_forest <- forest_mask2 == 0
  plot(non_forest, add = TRUE,
       col = adjustcolor("forestgreen", alpha.f = 0.2),
       legend = FALSE)

  legend("bottomright",
         inset = 0.02,
         legend = c("Low risk", "High risk", "Non-forest"),
         fill = c("forestgreen", "brown", adjustcolor("forestgreen", alpha.f = 0.3)),
         border = "grey30",
         bty = "o",
         title = "Forest Invasion Risk",
         bg = adjustcolor("white", alpha.f = 0.8))

  dev.off()
  message("  - Gradient risk map PDF saved (Mukura).")

  # --- Create summary table ---
  out_df <- data.frame(
    Site = "Mukura",
    Species = sp_name,
    Threshold = round(thr, 6),
    Forest_area_km2 = round(forest_area_km2, 2),
    Forest_risk_area_km2 = round(risk_area_km2, 2),
    Percent_risk = round(pct_risk, 2),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )

  # --- Export summary table ---
  csv_path <- file.path(outdir, paste0(sp_name, "_forest_risk_summary_Mukura.csv"))
  write.csv(out_df, csv_path, row.names = FALSE)
  message("  - Summary CSV exported to: ", csv_path)

  # --- Return both summary and raster
  return(list(summary = out_df, raster = risk_gradient))
}

```

```{r}
# Define Mukura-specific paths
outdir <- "/Users/blaise/Rwanda_National Parks/Code_Species_Modeling/Ensemble_Mukura_Figures"
if (!dir.exists(outdir)) dir.create(outdir, recursive = TRUE)

sp_name <- "Acacia_mearnsii"
res_mukura <- compute_forest_risk_gradient_mukura(sp_name, result_mukura, forest_mask, outdir)

# View and confirm
print(res_mukura$summary)


```

roc_obj is the ROC curve computed from your presence/absence (y) and
predictions (ens_pred).

best.method = "youden" finds the threshold that maximizes sensitivity +
specificity.

ret = "threshold" tells coords() to return the threshold value itself.

```{r}
library(terra)
library(pROC)
library(dplyr)

# ---- 1. Ensemble raster from results ----
ens_rast <- result_mukura$rasters$ENSEMBLE  # continuous habitat suitability (0–1)

# ---- 2. Derive max SSS threshold ----
# Use data and predictions already stored
y <- result_mukura$data_used$presence
ens_pred <- result_mukura$ensemble_cv  # cross-validated ensemble predictions

roc_obj <- pROC::roc(y, ens_pred)
sss_thresh <- pROC::coords(
  roc_obj, 
  x = "best", 
  ret = "threshold", 
  best.method = "youden" # max sensitivity + specificity
)




```

`roc_obj` is the **underlying ROC curve**, and `sss_thresh` is just one
point on that curve (the optimal threshold). Plotting shows the curve
with your chosen threshold for verification.

```{r}
plot(roc_obj, main = "ROC curve for Mukura ensemble")
abline(v = sss_thresh, col = "red", lty = 2)

```

```{r}
class(result_mukura$rasters$ENSEMBLE)

sss_thresh
```

```{r}
# ---- 3. Apply threshold to create binary invasion risk map ----
binary_risk <- ens_rast > sss_thresh

# ---- 4. Forest mask (if available) ----
# Replace this with your actual forest layer (aligned raster with 1=forest, 0=non-forest)

# Make sure NA handling is correct
forest_mask <- mask(forest_mask, result_mukura$rasters$ENSEMBLE)

```

```{r}

library(terra)

# Original ensemble raster
ensemble_raster <- result_mukura$rasters$ENSEMBLE

# Create binary raster: 1 if above threshold, 0 if below
binary_raster <- classify(ensemble_raster, 
                          rbind(c(-Inf, 0.1500127, 0),   # values <= threshold
                                c(0.1500127, Inf, 1)))   # values > threshold

# Apply forest mask (assuming 1 = forest, 0 = non-forest)
forest_risk <- mask(binary_raster, forest_mask, maskvalues = 0)
```

```{r}
 n_risk   <- global(forest_risk, "sum", na.rm = TRUE)
 pct_risk <- 100 * (n_risk / n_forest)
```

MAXENT ONLY


```{r}
# ============================================================
# 6) Wrapper for all species per site
# ============================================================
run_site_bundle <- function(site_name, predictors_stack, pres_list_xy, background_xy) {
  results <- list()
  for (nm in names(pres_list_xy)) {
    if (nrow(pres_list_xy[[nm]]) < 3) {
      warning(sprintf("[%s][%s] Too few occurrences (<3), skipping", site_name, nm))
      next
    }
    results[[nm]] <- run_sdm_species_site(
      site_name = site_name,
      species_name = nm,
      presence_xy = pres_list_xy[[nm]],
      background_xy = background_xy,
      predictors_stack = predictors_stack
    )
  }
  results
}

# ============================================================
# 7) Site-level mean suitability (across species)
# ============================================================
site_mean_maxent <- function(site_results_list) {
  rast_list <- lapply(site_results_list, function(z) z$raster)
  rast_list <- Filter(Negate(is.null), rast_list)
  if (length(rast_list) == 0) stop("No valid MaxEnt rasters found!")
  multi_rast <- rast_list[[1]]
  if (length(rast_list) > 1) {
    for (i in 2:length(rast_list)) multi_rast <- c(multi_rast, rast_list[[i]])
  }
  terra::app(multi_rast, fun = mean, na.rm = TRUE)
}

# ============================================================
# 8) Hotspot mapping
# ============================================================
make_hotspot <- function(rast, thr = 0.6) rast >= thr

```

UPDATES

```{r}
# ============================================================
# Load packages
# ============================================================
library(terra)
library(maxnet)
library(pROC)
library(PRROC)
library(caret)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(tibble)

# ============================================================
# 1) Helper: Extract environment safely
# ============================================================
extract_env <- function(xy, stack) {
  # If input is data.frame, convert to SpatVector
  if (inherits(xy, "data.frame")) {
    stop("Presence/background should be reprojected before calling extract_env()")
  }
  # Reproject to match stack CRS (just in case)
  if (!terra::crs(xy) == terra::crs(stack)) {
    xy <- terra::project(xy, stack)
  }
  # Extract values
  df <- terra::extract(stack, xy) |> as.data.frame()
  if ("ID" %in% names(df)) df <- dplyr::select(df, -ID)
  return(df)
}



# ---------------------------
# Clean predictors: drop NA-only, constant, highly collinear variables
# ---------------------------
clean_predictors <- function(X, cutoff = 0.999) {
  if (is.null(X) || ncol(X) == 0) return(X)

  # Convert factor/character to numeric (maxnet expects numeric predictors)
  for (nm in names(X)) {
    if (is.factor(X[[nm]]) || is.character(X[[nm]])) {
      X[[nm]] <- as.numeric(as.integer(X[[nm]]))
    }
  }

  # Drop NA-only or constant columns
  keep <- sapply(X, function(v) {
    if (all(is.na(v))) return(FALSE)
    if (length(unique(na.omit(v))) <= 1) return(FALSE)
    TRUE
  })
  X <- X[, keep, drop = FALSE]

  if (ncol(X) <= 1) return(X)

  # Drop highly correlated columns
  cor_mat <- suppressWarnings(cor(X, use = "pairwise.complete.obs"))
  drop <- caret::findCorrelation(cor_mat, cutoff = cutoff, verbose = FALSE)
  if (length(drop) > 0) X <- X[, -drop, drop = FALSE]

  return(X)
}

# ---------------------------
# CV fold maker: LOOCV if few presences, otherwise k-fold stratified
# ---------------------------
make_folds <- function(y, loocv_threshold = 30, k = 3) {
  n_pres <- sum(y == 1, na.rm = TRUE)
  if (n_pres < loocv_threshold) {
    list(method = "LOOCV", folds = seq_along(y))
  } else {
    set.seed(123)
    pos_idx <- which(y == 1); neg_idx <- which(y == 0)
    k <- min(k, length(pos_idx))
    pos_split <- split(pos_idx, sort(rank(runif(length(pos_idx))) %% k))
    neg_split <- split(neg_idx, sort(rank(runif(length(neg_idx))) %% k))
    folds <- vector("list", k)
    for (i in seq_len(k)) folds[[i]] <- c(pos_split[[i]], neg_split[[i]])
    list(method = "k-fold", folds = folds)
  }
}

# ---------------------------
# Metrics calculator (AUC, AUC-PR, TSS, Threshold using Youden)
# ---------------------------
calc_metrics <- function(obs, pred) {
  if (length(unique(obs)) < 2) {
    return(tibble(AUC = NA_real_, AUC_PR = NA_real_, TSS = NA_real_, Threshold = NA_real_))
  }
  roc_obj <- pROC::roc(obs, pred, quiet = TRUE)
  pr_obj  <- PRROC::pr.curve(scores.class0 = pred[obs == 1], scores.class1 = pred[obs == 0], curve = FALSE)
  thr <- pROC::coords(roc_obj, x = "best", best.method = "youden", ret = "threshold")
  bin <- as.integer(pred >= thr)
  TP <- sum(bin == 1 & obs == 1); TN <- sum(bin == 0 & obs == 0)
  FP <- sum(bin == 1 & obs == 0); FN <- sum(bin == 0 & obs == 1)
  sens <- ifelse((TP + FN) > 0, TP/(TP+FN), NA_real_)
  spec <- ifelse((TN + FP) > 0, TN/(TN+FP), NA_real_)
  tss  <- sens + spec - 1
  tibble::tibble(
    AUC = as.numeric(pROC::auc(roc_obj)),
    AUC_PR = ifelse(is.null(pr_obj$auc.integral), NA_real_, pr_obj$auc.integral),
    TSS = as.numeric(tss),
    Threshold = as.numeric(thr)
  )
}

# ---------------------------
# Debug helper
# ---------------------------
debug_maxnet <- function(y, X) {
  message("=== Debugging MaxEnt fit ===")
  message("Rows: ", nrow(X), " | Cols: ", ncol(X))
  message("Unique classes in y: ", paste(sort(unique(y)), collapse = ", "))
  na_cols <- names(X)[sapply(X, function(v) all(is.na(v)))]
  if (length(na_cols) > 0) message("NA-only columns: ", paste(na_cols, collapse = ", "))
  const_cols <- names(X)[sapply(X, function(v) length(unique(na.omit(v))) == 1)]
  if (length(const_cols) > 0) message("Constant columns: ", paste(const_cols, collapse = ", "))
  invisible(NULL)
}

# ---------------------------
# predict wrapper for terra::predict to call maxnet::predict
# terra will call FUN(model, newdata_row) with newdata being a data.frame/matrix of predictors
# ---------------------------

# wrapper for terra::predict
predict_maxnet <- function(model, newdata) {
  as.numeric(predict(model, newdata, type = "cloglog"))
}


# ---------------------------
# Core: run MaxEnt with CV & raster prediction
# - Returns: metrics (cv), final model, cv_predictions, final raster
# ---------------------------
run_sdm_species_site <- function(site_name,
                                 species_name,
                                 presence_xy,         # SpatVector (or data.frame x,y - see extract_env)
                                 background_xy,       # SpatVector (or data.frame x,y)
                                 predictors_stack,    # SpatRaster (terra)
                                 k = 3,               # k for k-fold (uses LOOCV if pres < 30)
                                 loocv_threshold = 30) {

  message(sprintf("[%s] %s", site_name, species_name))

  # 1) Extract env values (returns data.frames)
  pres_env <- extract_env(presence_xy, predictors_stack)
  back_env <- extract_env(background_xy, predictors_stack)

  message("Presences before drop_na: ", nrow(pres_env))
  message("Background before drop_na: ", nrow(back_env))

  # 2) Build modeling table
  pres_df <- cbind(pres_env, presence = 1L)
  back_df <- cbind(back_env, presence = 0L)
  model_data <- rbind(pres_df, back_df) %>% tidyr::drop_na()

  message("Combined after drop_na: ", nrow(model_data))
  message("Number of presences in model_data: ", sum(model_data$presence == 1))

  if (nrow(model_data) == 0) {
    warning("No model data after extraction -> returning NULL")
    return(NULL)
  }

  # Response & predictors
  y <- model_data$presence
  X <- model_data %>% dplyr::select(-presence)

  # Clean predictors (drop NA-only, constant, collinear)
  X <- clean_predictors(X)
  if (ncol(X) == 0) {
    warning("No usable predictors after cleaning -> returning NULL")
    return(NULL)
  }

  # debug
  debug_maxnet(y, X)

  # 3) CV folds
  folds_info <- make_folds(y, loocv_threshold = loocv_threshold, k = k)
  if (folds_info$method == "LOOCV") {
    folds <- lapply(seq_along(y), function(i) i)  # LOOCV: list of single-element test indices
  } else {
    folds <- folds_info$folds
  }

  # 4) CV loop: fit on train, predict on test
  preds_cv <- rep(NA_real_, length(y))
  for (fold_idx in seq_along(folds)) {
    test_idx <- folds[[fold_idx]]
    train_idx <- setdiff(seq_along(y), test_idx)

    X_train <- X[train_idx, , drop = FALSE]
    y_train <- y[train_idx]
    X_test  <- X[test_idx,  colnames(X_train), drop = FALSE]  # ensure same columns

    # If after cleaning train has no predictors, skip
    if (ncol(X_train) == 0) next

    # Fit maxnet on train
    mod <- tryCatch({
      maxnet::maxnet(p = y_train, data = X_train, f = maxnet::maxnet.formula(y_train, X_train, classes = "lqph"))
    }, error = function(e) {
      warning(sprintf("[%s][%s] maxnet fold fit error: %s", site_name, species_name, e$message))
      return(NULL)
    })
    if (is.null(mod)) next

   # Predict on test
pred_test <- tryCatch({
  as.numeric(predict(mod, X_test, type = "cloglog"))
}, error = function(e) {
  warning(sprintf("[%s][%s] maxnet fold predict error: %s", site_name, species_name, e$message))
  rep(NA_real_, nrow(X_test))
})

    preds_cv[test_idx] <- pred_test
  }

 

  # 5) Metrics from CV
  metrics <- calc_metrics(y, preds_cv)

  # If all NA or only one class in y after CV, bail
  if (all(is.na(preds_cv)) || length(unique(y)) < 2) {
    warning(sprintf("[%s][%s] CV produced no usable predictions or single-class response -> returning metrics only", site_name, species_name))
    return(list(metrics = metrics, cv_predictions = preds_cv, model = NULL, raster = NULL))
  }

  # 6) Final model on ALL data
  final_mod <- tryCatch({
    maxnet::maxnet(p = y, data = X, f = maxnet::maxnet.formula(y, X, classes = "lqph"))
  }, error = function(e) {
    warning(sprintf("[%s][%s] Final maxnet fit failed: %s", site_name, species_name, e$message))
    return(NULL)
  })

  if (is.null(final_mod)) {
    return(list(metrics = metrics, cv_predictions = preds_cv, model = NULL, raster = NULL))
  }

  # 7) Ensure raster layer names match predictors used by model
  #    IMPORTANT: this makes terra::predict feed columns to model in the right order
  names(predictors_stack) <- colnames(X)

  # 8) Predict raster using a wrapper fun that returns numeric vector per pixel
  pred_rast <- tryCatch({
    terra::predict(predictors_stack, final_mod, fun = predict_maxnet, na.rm = TRUE, cores = 1)
  }, error = function(e) {
    stop(sprintf("[%s][%s] terra::predict failed: %s", site_name, species_name, e$message))
  })

  # 9) Name output raster
  names(pred_rast) <- paste0(site_name, "_", species_name, "_MAXENT")
  #10
  # Variable importance
var_importance <- tryCatch({
  maxnet::maxnet.result(final_mod, X)
}, error = function(e) {
  warning(sprintf("[%s][%s] Variable importance failed: %s", site_name, species_name, e$message))
  NULL
})
  # 11) Return results
  return(list(
    metrics = metrics,             # CV-based metrics
    cv_predictions = preds_cv,
    model = final_mod,
    raster = pred_rast,
    data_used = model_data,
    variable_importance = var_importance
  ))
}

# ============================================================

```

```{r}
# ============================================================
# 6) Wrapper for all species per site
# ============================================================
run_site_bundle <- function(site_name, predictors_stack, pres_list_xy, background_xy) {
  results <- list()
  for (nm in names(pres_list_xy)) {
    if (nrow(pres_list_xy[[nm]]) < 3) {
      warning(sprintf("[%s][%s] Too few occurrences (<3), skipping", site_name, nm))
      next
    }
    results[[nm]] <- run_sdm_species_site(
      site_name = site_name,
      species_name = nm,
      presence_xy = pres_list_xy[[nm]],
      background_xy = background_xy,
      predictors_stack = predictors_stack
    )
  }
  results
}

# ============================================================
# 7) Site-level mean suitability (across species)
# ============================================================
site_mean_maxent <- function(site_results_list) {
  rast_list <- lapply(site_results_list, function(z) z$raster)
  rast_list <- Filter(Negate(is.null), rast_list)
  if (length(rast_list) == 0) stop("No valid MaxEnt rasters found!")
  multi_rast <- rast_list[[1]]
  if (length(rast_list) > 1) {
    for (i in 2:length(rast_list)) multi_rast <- c(multi_rast, rast_list[[i]])
  }
  terra::app(multi_rast, fun = mean, na.rm = TRUE)
}

# ============================================================
# 8) Hotspot mapping
# ============================================================
make_hotspot <- function(rast, thr = 0.6) rast >= thr

```

```{r}

# Example (after running results_* above): site-level hotspots
mean_gishwati <- site_mean_maxent(results_gishwati)
#mean_mukura   <- site_mean_maxent(results_mukura)

# ---- 10) Binary hotspot map at threshold t --------------------------------
# Example: fixed threshold = 0.6; you can also replace with Youden threshold from ROC
make_hotspot <- function(rast, thr = 0.6) rast >= thr

hotspot_gishwati <- make_hotspot(mean_gishwati, thr = 0.6)
#hotspot_mukura   <- make_hotspot(mean_mukura, thr = 0.6)
```

```{r}
plot(mean_gishwati, main = "Mean Suitability - Gishwati")
plot(hotspot_gishwati, main = "Hotspots - Gishwati (thr = 0.6)")

```

```{r}
# ---- 12) Quick suitability plot ----
 plot_suitability <- function(rast, site_name, species_name,
                             gishwati_vect, mukura_vect) {
  
  site_vect <- switch(
    site_name,
    "Gishwati" = gishwati_vect,
    "Mukura"   = mukura_vect,
    stop("Unknown site_name: must be 'Gishwati' or 'Mukura'")
  )
  
  tm_shape(rast) +
    tm_raster(title = "Suitability", palette = "YlOrRd", style = "cont", n = 10) +
    tm_shape(site_vect) +
    tm_borders(
      lwd = 2,
      col = ifelse(site_name == "Gishwati", "#298c8c", "#f1a226")
    ) +
    tm_layout(
      main.title = paste0(site_name, " - ", species_name, " (MaxEnt)"),
      legend.outside = TRUE
    )
}


```

```{r}


# ---- 11) Risk evaluation against forest mask (optional) -------------------
# forest_mask: SpatRaster with 1 = forest, 0 = non-forest
evaluate_risk <- function(maxent_raster, presence_xy, forest_mask = NULL, n_bg = 1000) {
  # Ensure presence is a SpatVector
  if (inherits(presence_xy, "SpatVector")) {
    pres_vec <- presence_xy
  } else if (is.data.frame(presence_xy)) {
    pres_vec <- terra::vect(presence_xy, geom = c("x", "y"), crs = crs(maxent_raster))
  } else {
    stop("presence_xy must be SpatVector or data.frame with x,y")
  }
  
  # ---- CRS check & reproject if needed ----
  if (!terra::same.crs(pres_vec, maxent_raster)) {
    message("Reprojecting presence points to match raster CRS...")
    pres_vec <- terra::project(pres_vec, crs(maxent_raster))
  }
  
  # Extract predictions for presences
  pres_vals <- terra::extract(maxent_raster, pres_vec)[[nlyr(maxent_raster)]]
  pres_vals <- pres_vals[!is.na(pres_vals)]
  
  # Sample background points inside raster extent
  bg_pts <- spatSample(maxent_raster, n_bg, method = "random", na.rm = TRUE, as.points = TRUE)
  bg_vals <- terra::extract(maxent_raster, bg_pts)[[nlyr(maxent_raster)]]
  bg_vals <- bg_vals[!is.na(bg_vals)]
  
  # If no valid values, skip
  if (length(pres_vals) == 0 || length(bg_vals) == 0) {
    warning("No valid predictions extracted for presences or background")
    return(NULL)
  }
  
  # Build response + predictions
  preds <- c(pres_vals, bg_vals)
  obs   <- c(rep(1L, length(pres_vals)), rep(0L, length(bg_vals)))
  
  # ROC evaluation
  roc_obj <- pROC::roc(obs, preds)
  threshold <- pROC::coords(roc_obj, "best", ret = "threshold", best.method = "youden")
  binary_map <- maxent_raster >= threshold
  
  # Optional forest mask overlay
  if (!is.null(forest_mask)) {
    if (!terra::same.crs(forest_mask, maxent_raster)) {
      message("Reprojecting forest_mask to match raster CRS...")
      forest_mask <- terra::project(forest_mask, crs(maxent_raster))
    }
    masked <- mask(binary_map, forest_mask, maskvalues = 0)
    percent_at_risk <- 100 * (global(masked, fun = "sum", na.rm = TRUE)[[1]] /
                                global(forest_mask, fun = "sum", na.rm = TRUE)[[1]])
  } else {
    percent_at_risk <- NA_real_
  }
  
  list(binary_map = binary_map, threshold = threshold, percent_at_risk = percent_at_risk)
}

```

```{r}
# ---- Forest mask from landcover raster -------------------
forest_codes <- c(25:49, 125)  # Stable Tree Cover, Disturbed Tree Cover, Wetland - Stable Tree

make_forest_mask <- function(landcover_rs, forest_codes) {
  # Initialize mask with 0
  mask_vals <- terra::classify(
    landcover_rs,
    rcl = matrix(cbind(unique(terra::values(landcover_rs)),
                       as.integer(unique(terra::values(landcover_rs)) %in% forest_codes)),
                 ncol = 2, byrow = FALSE)
  )
  names(mask_vals) <- "forest_mask"
  return(mask_vals)
}

forest_mask <- make_forest_mask(landcover_rs, forest_codes)

# Quick check
terra::plot(forest_mask, main = "Forest Mask (1 = forest, 0 = non-forest)")


```

```{r}
# 1) Check CRS
#crs(forest_mask)
#crs(gishwati_vect)

# 2) If CRS differ, reproject vector to match raster
if (!terra::same.crs(forest_mask, gishwati_vect)) {
  gishwati_vect <- terra::project(gishwati_vect, crs(forest_mask))
}

# 3) Crop raster
forest_mask_gishwati <- terra::crop(forest_mask, gishwati_vect)

if (!terra::same.crs(forest_mask, mukura_vect)) {
  mukura_vect <- terra::project(mukura_vect, crs(forest_mask))
}
forest_mask_mukura <- terra::crop(forest_mask, mukura_vect)


forest_mask_gishwati <- terra::mask(forest_mask_gishwati, gishwati_vect)
forest_mask_mukura   <- terra::mask(forest_mask_mukura, mukura_vect)
terra::plot(forest_mask_gishwati, main = "Forest Mask - Gishwati (1=forest, 0=non-forest)")
terra::plot(forest_mask_mukura, main = "Forest Mask - Mukura (1=forest, 0=non-forest)")
```

```{r}
# Input assumed: gmb_reserve_vect (SpatVector of reserve polygons)
stopifnot(exists("gmb_reserve_vect"))

# Convert to sf for filtering
gmb_reserve_sf <- sf::st_as_sf(gmb_reserve_vect)

# Split by source area (assuming column 'source' contains "Gishwati"/"Mukura")
gishwati_sf <- subset(gmb_reserve_sf, grepl("Gishwati", source))
mukura_sf   <- subset(gmb_reserve_sf, grepl("Mukura",  source))

# Dissolve to single polygon per site
gishwati_union <- sf::st_union(gishwati_sf)
mukura_union   <- sf::st_union(mukura_sf)

# Convert back to SpatVector for terra/tmap
gishwati_vect <- terra::vect(gishwati_union)
mukura_vect   <- terra::vect(mukura_union)
```

```{r}
plot_suitability <- function(rast, site_vect, site_name, species_name) {
  tm_shape(rast) +
    tm_raster(title = "Suitability", palette = "YlOrRd", style = "cont", n = 10) +
    tm_shape(site_vect) +
    tm_borders(lwd = 2, col = ifelse(site_name == "Gishwati", "#298c8c", "#f1a226")) +
    tm_layout(
      main.title = paste0(site_name, " - ", species_name, " (MaxEnt)"),
      legend.outside = TRUE
    )
}
```

```{r}
# Custom function to get variable importance safely for a single species
get_var_importance <- function(species_result) {
  if (is.null(species_result$model)) return(NULL)
  
  X <- species_result$data_used %>% dplyr::select(-presence)
  y <- species_result$data_used$presence
  
  # maxnet::var.importance expects model, X, y
  vi <- tryCatch({
    maxnet::var.importance(species_result$model, X, y)
  }, error = function(e) {
    warning("Variable importance failed: ", e$message)
    return(NULL)
  })
  
  return(vi)
}

# Example: loop over Gishwati species
vi_gishwati <- lapply(results_gishwati, get_var_importance)

# Example: loop over Mukura species
vi_mukura <- lapply(results_mukura, get_var_importance)

# Inspect variable importance for a species
vi_gishwati$Acacia_mearnsii
vi_mukura$Acacia_mearnsii

```

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(forcats)

# -----------------------------
# 1) Convert variable importance list to tidy data.frame
# -----------------------------
vi_to_df <- function(vi_list, site_name) {
  df_list <- lapply(names(vi_list), function(sp) {
    vi <- vi_list[[sp]]
    if (is.null(vi)) return(NULL)
    data.frame(
      Species = sp,
      Variable = names(vi),
      Importance = as.numeric(vi),
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    )
  })
  
  df <- do.call(rbind, df_list)
  if (!is.null(df)) df$Site <- site_name
  df
}

# -----------------------------
# 2) Example: Gishwati
# -----------------------------
vi_gishwati_df <- vi_to_df(vi_gishwati, site_name = "Gishwati")

# Make Species an ordered factor for plotting
vi_gishwati_df$Species <- fct_reorder(vi_gishwati_df$Species, vi_gishwati_df$Importance, .fun = max)

# -----------------------------
# 3) Plot heatmap
# -----------------------------
ggplot(vi_gishwati_df, aes(x = Variable, y = Species, fill = Importance)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white") +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "white", high = "#FF5733") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)) +
  labs(title = "Variable Importance Heatmap - Gishwati",
       x = "Environmental Variable",
       y = "Species",
       fill = "Importance")

```

```{r}
vi_mukura_df <- vi_to_df(vi_mukura, site_name = "Mukura")

ggplot(vi_mukura_df, aes(x = Variable, y = Species, fill = Importance)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white") +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "white", high = "#298c8c") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)) +
  labs(title = "Variable Importance Heatmap - Mukura",
       x = "Environmental Variable",
       y = "Species",
       fill = "Importance")

```

####SKIP#####\
Check raster layers

```{r}
library(terra)

# Identify which layers have only NA values
na_layers <- sapply(1:nlyr(stack_valid_trimmed), function(i) {
  all(is.na(values(stack_valid_trimmed[[i]])))
})
which(na_layers)  # Indices of problematic layers
names(stack_valid_trimmed)[na_layers]  # Names of those layers

```

Filter Out Points Falling on NA Raster Values

```{r}
library(terra)
library(raster)

# Convert presence/background points to SpatVector (terra-friendly format)
presence_vect <- vect(presence_points, geom = c("Central_coordinate_Longitude", "Central_coordinate_Latitude"), crs = crs(stack_valid_trimmed))
background_vect <- vect(background_points, geom = c("Central_coordinate_Longitude", "Central_coordinate_Latitude"), crs = crs(stack_valid_trimmed))

# Extract values at point locations
pres_vals <- terra::extract(stack_valid_trimmed, presence_vect)
back_vals <- terra::extract(stack_valid_trimmed, background_vect)

# Filter out rows with any NA values
valid_pres <- complete.cases(pres_vals)
valid_back <- complete.cases(back_vals)

# Keep only valid points
presence_clean <- presence_points[valid_pres, ]
background_clean <- background_points[valid_back, ]

```

How man points were removed due to to fall on NA raster value

```{r}
cat("Dropped", sum(!valid_pres), "presence points and", sum(!valid_back), "background points due to NA values.\n")

```

ou're losing **over 60%** of your presence data — that's significant. To
**avoid or reduce** points falling on `NA` raster values, here are
several **actionable strategies**:

Ensure Raster Extent Covers All Points

```{r}
extent_points <- ext(vect(presence_points, geom = c("Central_coordinate_Longitude", "Central_coordinate_Latitude")))
extent_rasters <- ext(stack_current)

overlap <- intersect(extent_points, extent_rasters)
print(overlap)

```

ABOVE CODE SHOW THAT THERE IS NO VERLAPP

Check CRS of Both

```{r}
crs_points <- crs(vect(presence_points, geom = c("Central_coordinate_Longitude", "Central_coordinate_Latitude")))
crs_rasters <- crs(stack_valid_trimmed)

print(crs_points)
print(crs_rasters)

```

Points did not have CRS

### Assign CRS to Points, Then Reproject

Assuming your presence points are in **WGS84 geographic coordinates**
(`EPSG:4326`, i.e., longitude/latitude), here's how to fix it:

```{r}
# Load necessary libraries
library(terra)

# Step 1: Convert to `SpatVector` with correct CRS (WGS84, degrees)
presence_vect <- vect(presence_points, geom = c("Central_coordinate_Longitude", "Central_coordinate_Latitude"), crs = "EPSG:4326")

# Step 2: Reproject points to match raster CRS
presence_vect_proj <- project(presence_vect, crs(stack_valid_trimmed))

# OPTIONAL: Repeat for background points if needed
background_vect <- vect(background_points, geom = c("Central_coordinate_Longitude", "Central_coordinate_Latitude"), crs = "EPSG:4326")
background_vect_proj <- project(background_vect, crs(stack_valid_trimmed))

```

#Filter presence and background points within the study polygon

```{r}
presence_clipped <- presence_vect_proj[gmb_reserve_vect, ]
background_clipped <- background_vect_proj[gmb_reserve_vect, ]


```

To avoid warning

```{r}
terra::global(stack_current, fun = function(x) all(is.na(x)))

```

```{r}
terra::global(stack_current, fun = min, na.rm = TRUE)
terra::global(stack_current, fun = max, na.rm = TRUE)

```

```{r}
terra::global(stack_valid_trimmed, fun = min, na.rm = TRUE)
terra::global(stack_valid_trimmed, fun = max, na.rm = TRUE)

```

#convert to raster

```{r}
library(raster)
stack_current_raster <- raster::stack(stack_current)  # from terra to raster

```

```{r}
# Load required packages
library(terra)
library(sf)

# --- Step 1: Define target CRS for Rwanda (UTM Zone 35S) ---
rwanda_crs <- "EPSG:32735"

# --- Step 2: Reproject study area ---
gmb_reserve_proj <- st_transform(gmb_reserve, crs = rwanda_crs)
gmb_reserve_vect <- vect(gmb_reserve_proj)  # Convert to SpatVector

# --- Step 3: Convert RasterStack (from raster) to SpatRaster ---
stack_current_terra <- rast(stack_current_raster)

# --- Step 4: Reproject rasters to Rwanda CRS ---
stack_current_proj <- project(stack_current_terra, rwanda_crs)



```

#used this code

```{r}
# --- Step 5: Reproject presence/background points to match raster CRS ---
# These should be in sf format initially
# Correct projection using terra::project()
presence_sp <- project(presence_clipped, rwanda_crs)
background_sp <- project(background_clipped, rwanda_crs)



# Step 6: Extract raster values at presence locations
extract_vals <- extract(stack_current_proj, presence_sp)

# Optional: remove rows with NA (if needed for modeling)
presence_vals_clean <- na.omit(presence_vals)


# --- Step 6: Extract raster values at presence locations ---
extract_vals <- extract(stack_current_proj, presence_sp)

# Optional: check for NA values in any layer at those points
has_na <- apply(extract_vals[ , -1], 1, function(x) any(is.na(x)))
table(has_na)
```

#not used

Run model with bias

```{r}
current_model <- maxent(
  x = stack_current_raster,
  p = presence_sp,
  a = background_sp,
  args = c(
    "biasfile=bias_mask.asc",
    "jackknife=true", 
    "responsecurves=true", 
    "replicates=1", 
    "randomseed=true"
  )
)

```

#Above code has error. Let Maxent generate background points itself.
This allows the use of the biasfile

#Reproject\

Make sure your background points do not fall on NA cells in any raster
layer.

```{r}

library(raster)  # required for RasterStack

# Convert SpatRaster to RasterStack
stack_current_proj_raster <- raster::stack(stack_current_proj)

# Now run maxent This model did not work because of biasfile
current_model <- maxent(
  x = stack_current_proj,
  p = presence_sp,
  a = background_sp,    # <-- include background here!
  args = c(
    "biasfile=bias_mask.asc",
    "jackknife=true",
    "responsecurves=true",
    "replicates=1",
    "randomseed=true"
  )
)


```

try without biasfile , stack_raster was defined below

```{r}
current_model <- maxent(
  x = stack_raster,
  p = presence_sp,
  args = c(
    "jackknife=true",
    "responsecurves=true",
    "replicates=1",
    "randomseed=true"
  )
)

```

or Don't use `biasfile`, and keep your own background points\
Update code above by first Convert `SpatRaster` back to `RasterStack`

```{r}
library(raster)
stack_raster <- stack(stack_current_proj)  # From terra::SpatRaster to raster::RasterStack

```

Convert presence/background from SpatVector to SpatialPoints

```{r}
presence_sp <- as(presence_sp, "Spatial")
background_sp <- as(background_sp, "Spatial")

```

```{r}
current_model2 <- maxent(
  x =  stack_raster,
  p = presence_sp,
  a = background_sp,
  args = c(
    "jackknife=true", 
    "responsecurves=true", 
    "replicates=1", 
    "randomseed=true"
  )
)

```

```{r}
# Plot jackknife test

plot(current_model2)

```

Explain both models out and why to use one:

#### **Use Model 1 (with background points)** IF:

Your background points are **carefully sampled** (e.g., spatially
stratified, within the same survey area).You're trying to control for
**sampling bias**, or your environmental layers cover a broader region
than just the area sur You don’t use a `biasfile`.

-   **Use Model 2 (no background, Maxent samples)** IF:

-   You **don’t have reliable background sampling**, and you just want
    Maxent to handle it. Your predictor layers are already **clipped
    tightly to the study region**, so Maxent won’t sample unrelated
    areas.

    You want to use a `biasfile` to restrict sampling.

To **assess the relative importance of each predictor variable** in
explaining the species' presence/absence using **MaxEnt's Jackknife
test**.

This helps you **select the most influential environmental variables**
before running a full model — avoiding overfitting and improving model
interpretability

Check for species specific, this code include Snapping. Use the code
below\

```{r}
library(dismo)
library(terra)
library(tidyverse)

# Define CRS as string (always safer for terra)
rwanda_crs <- "EPSG:32735"

species_list <- c("Acacia_mearnsii", "Acacia_melanoxylon", "Alnus_acuminata")
contribution_list <- list()

for (sp in species_list) {
  cat("Running model for:", sp, "\n")
  
  # Extract presence/background points
  sp_data <- fia_csv1 %>%
    dplyr::select(Central_coordinate_Longitude, Central_coordinate_Latitude, all_of(paste0(sp, "_presence"))) %>%
    rename(presence = paste0(sp, "_presence")) %>%
    na.omit()
  
  presence_points <- sp_data[sp_data$presence == 1, c("Central_coordinate_Longitude", "Central_coordinate_Latitude")]
  background_points <- maxent_data[maxent_data$any_species_presence == 0, c("Central_coordinate_Longitude", "Central_coordinate_Latitude")]
  
  presence_vect <- vect(presence_points, geom = c("Central_coordinate_Longitude", "Central_coordinate_Latitude"), crs = rwanda_crs)
  background_vect <- vect(background_points, geom = c("Central_coordinate_Longitude", "Central_coordinate_Latitude"), crs = rwanda_crs)
  
  # Use character string for CRS
  raster_crs <- crs(stack_valid_trimmed, proj = TRUE)
  
  presence_vect_proj <- project(presence_vect, raster_crs)
  background_vect_proj <- project(background_vect, raster_crs)

  # Snap points to nearest valid raster cell
  snap_to_valid_cell <- function(points_vect, raster, max_distance = 28) {
    valid_cells <- which(!is.na(values(raster[[1]])))
    coords <- xyFromCell(raster, valid_cells)
    valid_vect <- vect(coords, crs = crs(raster, proj = TRUE))
    
    snapped_points <- lapply(1:nrow(points_vect), function(i) {
      point <- points_vect[i, ]
      dists <- distance(point, valid_vect)
      if (all(is.na(dists))) return(NA)
      min_dist <- min(dists, na.rm = TRUE)
      if (min_dist <= max_distance) {
        nearest_idx <- which.min(dists)
        return(coords[nearest_idx, ])
      } else {
        return(NA)
      }
    })
    
    snapped_coords <- do.call(rbind, snapped_points)
    snapped_coords[complete.cases(snapped_coords), , drop = FALSE]
  }

  presence_clean <- snap_to_valid_cell(presence_vect_proj, stack_valid_trimmed)
  background_clean <- snap_to_valid_cell(background_vect_proj, stack_valid_trimmed)

  # Only model if enough valid points were found
  if (!is.null(presence_clean) && !is.null(background_clean) &&
      nrow(presence_clean) >= 20 && nrow(background_clean) >= 20) {

    model <- maxent(
      x = raster::stack(stack_valid_trimmed),
      p = presence_clean,
      a = background_clean,
      args = c("jackknife=true", "responsecurves=true", "replicates=1", "randomseed=true")
    )

    contrib_df <- model@results

    # Extract variable contribution and gain drop
    contrib_rows <- grep("\\.contribution$", rownames(contrib_df))
    gain_rows <- grep("^Training\\.gain\\.without\\.", rownames(contrib_df))

    if (length(contrib_rows) > 0 && length(gain_rows) > 0) {
      contrib_vars <- contrib_df[contrib_rows, , drop = FALSE]
      contrib_vars <- as.data.frame(contrib_vars)
      contrib_vars$variable <- gsub("\\.contribution$", "", rownames(contrib_vars))
      colnames(contrib_vars)[1] <- "percent_contribution"

      gain_df <- contrib_df[gain_rows, , drop = FALSE]
      gain_df <- as.data.frame(gain_df)
      gain_df$variable <- sub("^Training\\.gain\\.without\\.", "", rownames(gain_df))
      colnames(gain_df)[1] <- "gain_without"

      merged <- merge(contrib_vars, gain_df, by = "variable")
      merged$species <- sp

      if ("Regularized.training.gain" %in% rownames(contrib_df)) {
        overall_gain <- contrib_df["Regularized.training.gain", 1]
        merged$gain_drop_pct <- 100 * (overall_gain - merged$gain_without) / overall_gain
      } else {
        merged$gain_drop_pct <- NA
      }

      contribution_list[[sp]] <- merged
    } else {
      cat("⚠️ Could not extract contribution data for", sp, "\n")
    }

  } else {
    cat("⚠️ Not enough valid points for", sp, "\n")
  }
}

```

```{r}
# Combine all results into one data frame
all_contributions <- bind_rows(contribution_list)
View(all_contributions)
```

We use this code: Loop per species

```{r}
# Load libraries
library(terra)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(dismo)
library(raster)  # Needed because dismo::maxent requires RasterStack

# Define UTM projection for Rwanda (zone 35S)
utm_crs <- "EPSG:32735"

# Ensure stack_valid_trimmed is a SpatRaster, then convert to RasterStack
if (!inherits(stack_valid_trimmed, "SpatRaster")) {
  stack_valid_trimmed <- rast(stack_valid_trimmed)
}
stack_raster <- raster::stack(stack_valid_trimmed)  # for dismo compatibility

# Define species list
species_list <- c("Acacia_mearnsii", "Acacia_melanoxylon", "Alnus_acuminata")
contribution_list <- list()

# Loop through species
for (sp in species_list) {
  cat("\n🔍 Running model for:", sp, "\n")
  
  # Prepare presence/background data
  sp_data <- fia_csv1 %>%
    select(Central_coordinate_Longitude, Central_coordinate_Latitude, !!sym(paste0(sp, "_presence"))) %>%
    rename(presence = !!sym(paste0(sp, "_presence"))) %>%
    drop_na()
  
  presence_points <- sp_data %>%
    filter(presence == 1) %>%
    select(Central_coordinate_Longitude, Central_coordinate_Latitude)
  
  background_points <- maxent_data %>%
    filter(any_species_presence == 0) %>%
    select(Central_coordinate_Longitude, Central_coordinate_Latitude)
  
  if (nrow(presence_points) >= 20 && nrow(background_points) >= 20) {
    
    # Convert to SpatVector with lat/lon CRS
    presence_vect <- vect(presence_points, geom = c("Central_coordinate_Longitude", "Central_coordinate_Latitude"), crs = "EPSG:4326")
    background_vect <- vect(background_points, geom = c("Central_coordinate_Longitude", "Central_coordinate_Latitude"), crs = "EPSG:4326")
    
    # Reproject to raster CRS (if needed)
    presence_vect <- project(presence_vect, crs(stack_valid_trimmed))
    background_vect <- project(background_vect, crs(stack_valid_trimmed))
    
    # Extract raster values at locations
    presence_vals <- terra::extract(stack_valid_trimmed, presence_vect)
    background_vals <- terra::extract(stack_valid_trimmed, background_vect)
    
    # Remove NAs (drop ID column)
    valid_presence <- complete.cases(presence_vals[, -1])
    valid_background <- complete.cases(background_vals[, -1])
    
    # Filter coords
    presence_coords <- crds(presence_vect, df = TRUE)[valid_presence, ]
    background_coords <- crds(background_vect, df = TRUE)[valid_background, ]
    
    cat("✅ Valid presence:", nrow(presence_coords), "| background:", nrow(background_coords), "\n")
    
    if (nrow(presence_coords) >= 20 && nrow(background_coords) >= 20) {
      
      # Run Maxent
      model <- dismo::maxent(
        x = stack_valid_trimmed,
        p = presence_coords,
        a = background_coords,
        args = c("jackknife=true", "responsecurves=true", "replicates=1", "randomseed=true")
      )
      
      if (!is.null(model@results) && nrow(model@results) > 0) {
        results_vec <- model@results[, 1]
        names(results_vec) <- rownames(model@results)
        
        contrib_names <- grep("\\.contribution$", names(results_vec), value = TRUE)
        gain_names <- grep("^Training\\.gain\\.without\\.", names(results_vec), value = TRUE)
        
        if (length(contrib_names) > 0 && length(gain_names) > 0) {
          contrib_vars <- data.frame(
            variable = gsub("\\.contribution$", "", contrib_names),
            percent_contribution = results_vec[contrib_names],
            row.names = NULL
          )
          
          gain_df <- data.frame(
            variable = gsub("^Training\\.gain\\.without\\.", "", gain_names),
            gain_without = results_vec[gain_names],
            row.names = NULL
          )
          
          merged <- merge(contrib_vars, gain_df, by = "variable")
          merged$species <- sp
          
          if ("Regularized.training.gain" %in% names(results_vec)) {
            overall_gain <- results_vec["Regularized.training.gain"]
            merged$gain_drop_pct <- 100 * (overall_gain - merged$gain_without) / overall_gain
          } else {
            merged$gain_drop_pct <- NA
          }
          
          contribution_list[[sp]] <- merged
          cat("✅ Model completed and data extracted for", sp, "\n")
        } else {
          cat("⚠️ Contribution or gain data missing for", sp, "\n")
        }
      } else {
        cat("❌ Maxent model failed or returned empty results for", sp, "\n")
      }
      
    } else {
      cat("⚠️ Too few valid points after NA filtering for", sp, "\n")
    }
    
  } else {
    cat("⚠️ Not enough initial presence/background points for", sp, "\n")
  }
}

```

#update code

```{r}
# Load libraries
library(terra)
library(raster)
library(dismo)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(sp)

# Define UTM projection for Rwanda (zone 35S)
utm_crs <- "+proj=utm +zone=35 +south +datum=WGS84 +units=m +no_defs"

# Step 1: Convert SpatRaster -> RasterStack
stack_valid_trimmed_r <- raster::stack(stack_valid_trimmed)

# Step 2: Define species list
species_list <- c("Acacia_mearnsii", "Acacia_melanoxylon", "Alnus_acuminata")
contribution_list <- list()

# Step 3: Loop through species
for (sp in species_list) {
  cat("\n🔍 Running model for:", sp, "\n")
  
  # Prepare presence/background data
  sp_data <- fia_csv1 %>%
    select(Central_coordinate_Longitude, Central_coordinate_Latitude, !!sym(paste0(sp, "_presence"))) %>%
    rename(presence = !!sym(paste0(sp, "_presence"))) %>%
    drop_na()
  
  presence_points <- sp_data %>%
    filter(presence == 1) %>%
    select(Central_coordinate_Longitude, Central_coordinate_Latitude)
  
  background_points <- maxent_data %>%
    filter(any_species_presence == 0) %>%
    select(Central_coordinate_Longitude, Central_coordinate_Latitude)
  
  # Check raw count
  if (nrow(presence_points) >= 20 && nrow(background_points) >= 20) {
    
    # Convert to SpatialPoints (WGS84)
    presence_sp <- SpatialPoints(presence_points, proj4string = CRS("+proj=longlat +datum=WGS84"))
    background_sp <- SpatialPoints(background_points, proj4string = CRS("+proj=longlat +datum=WGS84"))
    
    # Reproject to raster CRS
    raster_crs <- projection(stack_valid_trimmed_r)
    presence_sp <- spTransform(presence_sp, CRS(raster_crs))
    background_sp <- spTransform(background_sp, CRS(raster_crs))
    
    # Extract raster values
    presence_vals <- raster::extract(stack_valid_trimmed_r, presence_sp)
    background_vals <- raster::extract(stack_valid_trimmed_r, background_sp)
    
    # Find valid (non-NA) indices
    valid_presence_idx <- which(complete.cases(presence_vals))
    valid_background_idx <- which(complete.cases(background_vals))
    
    # Subset SpatialPoints safely
    presence_sp <- presence_sp[valid_presence_idx]
    background_sp <- background_sp[valid_background_idx]
    
    cat("✅ Valid presence:", length(valid_presence_idx),
        "| background:", length(valid_background_idx), "\n")
    
    if (length(valid_presence_idx) >= 20 && length(valid_background_idx) >= 20) {
      
      # Run Maxent
      model <- dismo::maxent(
        x = stack_valid_trimmed_r,
        p = presence_sp,
        a = background_sp,
        args = c("jackknife=true", "responsecurves=true", "replicates=1", "randomseed=true")
      )
      
      # Check model results
      if (!is.null(model@results) && nrow(model@results) > 0) {
        results_vec <- model@results[, 1]
        names(results_vec) <- rownames(model@results)
        
        # Extract contribution and gain
        contrib_names <- grep("\\.contribution$", names(results_vec), value = TRUE)
        gain_names <- grep("^Training\\.gain\\.without\\.", names(results_vec), value = TRUE)
        
        if (length(contrib_names) > 0 && length(gain_names) > 0) {
          contrib_vars <- data.frame(
            variable = gsub("\\.contribution$", "", contrib_names),
            percent_contribution = results_vec[contrib_names],
            row.names = NULL
          )
          
          gain_df <- data.frame(
            variable = gsub("^Training\\.gain\\.without\\.", "", gain_names),
            gain_without = results_vec[gain_names],
            row.names = NULL
          )
          
          merged <- merge(contrib_vars, gain_df, by = "variable")
          merged$species <- sp
          
          if ("Regularized.training.gain" %in% names(results_vec)) {
            overall_gain <- results_vec["Regularized.training.gain"]
            merged$gain_drop_pct <- 100 * (overall_gain - merged$gain_without) / overall_gain
          } else {
            merged$gain_drop_pct <- NA
          }
          
          contribution_list[[sp]] <- merged
          cat("✅ Model completed and data extracted for", sp, "\n")
        } else {
          cat("⚠️ Contribution or gain data missing for", sp, "\n")
        }
        
      } else {
        cat("❌ Maxent model failed or returned empty results for", sp, "\n")
      }
      
    } else {
      cat("⚠️ Too few valid points after filtering for", sp, "\n")
    }
    
  } else {
    cat("⚠️ Not enough initial presence/background points for", sp, "\n")
  }
}

```

```{r}
# Combine all species results into one data frame (if needed)
final_contributions <- bind_rows(contribution_list)

```

\

```{r}
library(dismo)
library(raster)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)

# Replace with your species list
species_list <- c("Acacia_mearnsii", "Acacia_melanoxylon", "Alnus_acuminata")
contribution_list <- list()

# ✅ Convert to raster::RasterStack to avoid projection issues
stack_valid_trimmed <- raster::stack(stack_valid_trimmed)

# Loop over each species
for (sp in species_list) {
  cat("Running model for:", sp, "\n")
  
  # Get presence data
  sp_data <- fia_csv1 %>%
    select(Central_coordinate_Longitude, Central_coordinate_Latitude, !!sym(paste0(sp, "_presence"))) %>%
    rename(presence = !!sym(paste0(sp, "_presence"))) %>%
    drop_na()
  
  # Separate presence and background points
  presence_points <- sp_data %>%
    filter(presence == 1) %>%
    select(Central_coordinate_Longitude, Central_coordinate_Latitude)
  
  background_points <- maxent_data %>%
    filter(any_species_presence == 0) %>%
    select(Central_coordinate_Longitude, Central_coordinate_Latitude)
  
  # Check minimum raw point counts
  if (nrow(presence_points) >= 20 && nrow(background_points) >= 20) {
    
    # Create SpatialPoints with known CRS
    utm_crs <- "+proj=utm +zone=35 +south +datum=WGS84 +units=m +no_defs"
    presence_sp <- sp::SpatialPoints(presence_points, proj4string = sp::CRS(utm_crs))
    background_sp <- sp::SpatialPoints(background_points, proj4string = sp::CRS(utm_crs))
    
    # ✅ Ensure the projection string is valid
    raster_crs_str <- as.character(raster::projection(stack_valid_trimmed))
    if (is.null(raster_crs_str) || raster_crs_str == "") {
      stop("❌ Raster CRS is NULL or empty. Please check stack_valid_trimmed.")
    }
    
    # Reproject points to raster CRS
    presence_sp <- sp::spTransform(presence_sp, sp::CRS(raster_crs_str))
    background_sp <- sp::spTransform(background_sp, sp::CRS(raster_crs_str))
    
    # Filter out NAs from raster extraction
    presence_vals <- raster::extract(stack_valid_trimmed, presence_sp)
    valid_presence_idx <- complete.cases(presence_vals)
    presence_sp <- presence_sp[valid_presence_idx, ]
    
    background_vals <- raster::extract(stack_valid_trimmed, background_sp)
    valid_background_idx <- complete.cases(background_vals)
    background_sp <- background_sp[valid_background_idx, ]
    
    # Check filtered point counts again
    if (length(presence_sp) >= 20 && length(background_sp) >= 20) {
      
      # Run Maxent model
      model <- dismo::maxent(
        x = stack_valid_trimmed,
        p = presence_sp,
        a = background_sp,
        args = c("jackknife=true", "responsecurves=true", "replicates=1", "randomseed=true")
      )
      
      contrib_df <- model@results
      contrib_rows <- grep("\\.contribution$", rownames(contrib_df))
      gain_rows <- grep("^Training\\.gain\\.without\\.", rownames(contrib_df))
      
      if (length(contrib_rows) > 0 && length(gain_rows) > 0) {
        contrib_vars <- as.data.frame(contrib_df[contrib_rows, , drop = FALSE])
        contrib_vars$variable <- gsub("\\.contribution$", "", rownames(contrib_vars))
        colnames(contrib_vars)[1] <- "percent_contribution"
        
        gain_df <- as.data.frame(contrib_df[gain_rows, , drop = FALSE])
        gain_df$variable <- gsub("^Training\\.gain\\.without\\.", "", rownames(gain_df))
        colnames(gain_df)[1] <- "gain_without"
        
        merged <- merge(contrib_vars, gain_df, by = "variable")
        merged$species <- sp
        
        if ("Regularized.training.gain" %in% rownames(contrib_df)) {
          overall_gain <- contrib_df["Regularized.training.gain", 1]
          merged$gain_drop_pct <- 100 * (overall_gain - merged$gain_without) / overall_gain
        } else {
          merged$gain_drop_pct <- NA
        }
        
        contribution_list[[sp]] <- merged
      } else {
        cat("⚠️ No contribution data for", sp, "\n")
      }
      
    } else {
      cat("⚠️ Too many NA values after raster extraction for", sp, "\n")
    }
    
  } else {
    cat("⚠️ Not enough valid raw points for", sp, "\n")
  }
}

```

```{r}
# Combine all results into one data frame
# Combine all results
all_contributions <- dplyr::bind_rows(contribution_list)
View(all_contributions)

```

```{r}

# Heatmap of variable contributions across species
library(ggplot2)

ggplot(all_contributions, aes(x = species, y = reorder(variable, percent_contribution), fill = percent_contribution)) +
  geom_tile() +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(name = "% Contribution") +
  labs(title = "Variable Contributions by Species", x = "Species", y = "Variable") +
  theme_minimal()

```

```{r}
library(ggplot2)

# Create Figures directory if it doesn't exist
fig_dir <- "/Users/blaise/Rwanda_National Parks/Code_Species_Modeling/Figures"
if (!dir.exists(fig_dir)) {
  dir.create(fig_dir, recursive = TRUE)
}

# Create the plot object
contrib_plot <- ggplot(all_contributions, aes(x = species, y = reorder(variable, percent_contribution), fill = percent_contribution)) +
  geom_tile() +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(name = "% Contribution") +
  labs(title = "Variable Contributions by Species", x = "Species", y = "Variable") +
  theme_minimal()

# Save as PNG (high resolution)
ggsave(filename = file.path(fig_dir, "variable_contributions.png"),
       plot = contrib_plot, device = "png", dpi = 600, width = 10, height = 8, units = "in")

# Save as PDF (vector quality)
ggsave(filename = file.path(fig_dir, "variable_contributions.pdf"),
       plot = contrib_plot, device = "pdf", width = 10, height = 8)

```

UPDATE CODE

MaxEnt with NA Imputation and Visualization

```{r}

# === 1. Load libraries ===
library(terra)
library(dismo)
library(tidyverse)
library(gstat)
library(sp)
library(ggplot2)

# === 2. Define Rwanda UTM CRS (adjust zone if needed) ===
rwanda_crs <- "EPSG:32736"  # Use EPSG:32737 if eastern zone applies

# === 3. Kriging-based NA fill function ===
fill_na_kriging <- function(r) {
  pts <- terra::as.data.frame(terra::as.points(r), na.rm = FALSE)
  colnames(pts) <- c("x","y","value")
  known <- pts[!is.na(pts$value), ]; unknown <- pts[is.na(pts$value), ]
  coordinates(known) <- ~x + y; coordinates(unknown) <- ~x + y
  vgm1 <- variogram(value ~ 1, known)
  vgm_fit <- fit.variogram(vgm1, model = vgm("Sph"))
  kriged <- krige(value ~ 1, known, unknown, vgm_fit)
  pts$value[is.na(pts$value)] <- kriged$var1.pred
  rast_xyz <- rast(pts, type = "xyz", crs = terra::crs(r))
  return(resample(rast_xyz, r))
}

# === 4. Fill all layers ===
stack_current <- rast(stack_valid_trimmed)  # your prepared SpatRaster stack
filled_list <- map(1:nlyr(stack_current), ~{
  cat("Kriging for:", names(stack_current)[.x], "\n")
  fill_na_kriging(stack_current[[.x]])
})
stack_filled <- rast(filled_list)
names(stack_filled) <- names(stack_current)

# Verify no NAs remain
print(terra::global(stack_filled, fun = function(x) sum(is.na(x))))


```

```{r}
# === 5. Species presence background prep ===
fia_csv1 <- fia_csv %>%
  mutate(Acacia_mearnsii = ifelse(Species_1_Acacia_mearnsii_Perc > 0,1,0),
         Acacia_melanoxylon = ifelse(Species_2_Acacia_melanoxylon_Perc > 0,1,0),
         Alnus_acuminata = ifelse(Species_3_Alnus_acuminata_Perc > 0,1,0))

species_list <- c("Acacia_mearnsii","Acacia_melanoxylon","Alnus_acuminata")
contrib_list <- list()

# === 6. Modeling loop ===
for(sp in species_list) {
  message("Running: ", sp)
  spdat <- fia_csv1 %>% drop_na(Central_coordinate_Longitude, Central_coordinate_Latitude, all_of(sp))
  pres_pts <- spdat %>% filter(!!sym(sp)==1) %>% select(Longitude=Central_coordinate_Longitude, Latitude=Central_coordinate_Latitude)
  bgdat <- fia_csv1 %>% filter(Acacia_mearnsii + Acacia_melanoxylon + Alnus_acuminata == 0) %>% 
           select(Longitude=Central_coordinate_Longitude, Latitude=Central_coordinate_Latitude)
  pres_vect <- vect(pres_pts, geom=c("Longitude","Latitude"), crs="EPSG:4326") %>% project(rwanda_crs)
  bg_vect   <- vect(bgdat, crs="EPSG:4326") %>% project(rwanda_crs)
  snap_fn <- function(points_vect) {
    valid <- terra::cells(stack_filled, 1)[[1]]
    xyc <- terra::xyFromCell(stack_filled, valid)
    valid_vect <- vect(xyc, crs=rwanda_crs)
    do.call(rbind, lapply(1:nrow(points_vect), function(i){
      d <- terra::distance(points_vect[i,], valid_vect)
      idx <- which.min(d)
      if(d[idx] <= 28) xyc[idx,] else NA
    })) %>% na.omit()
  }
  pres_snapped <- snap_fn(pres_vect)
  bg_snapped   <- snap_fn(bg_vect)
  if(nrow(pres_snapped) >= 20 && nrow(bg_snapped)>=20) {
    model <- maxent(x = raster::stack(stack_filled), p = pres_snapped, a = bg_snapped,
                    args = c("jackknife=true", "responsecurves=true", "replicates=1"))
    res <- model@results
    contr <- as.data.frame(res[grep("\\.contribution$",rownames(res)),,drop=FALSE])
    contr$variable <- gsub("\\.contribution$","",rownames(contr))
    colnames(contr)[1] <- "percent_contribution"
    gain <- as.data.frame(res[grep("^Training\\.gain\\.without\\.",rownames(res)),,drop=FALSE])
    gain$variable <- gsub("^Training\\.gain\\.without\\.","",rownames(gain))
    colnames(gain)[1] <- "gain_without"
    merged <- merge(contr,gain,by="variable")
    merged$species <- sp
    overall <- if("Regularized.training.gain"%in%rownames(res)) res["Regularized.training.gain",1] else NA
    merged$gain_drop_pct <- if(!is.na(overall)) 100*(overall - merged$gain_without)/overall else NA
    contrib_list[[sp]] <- merged
  } else {
    warning("Insufficient points for ",sp)
  }
}

# === 7. Visualize contributions ===
all_contrib <- bind_rows(contrib_list)
p <- ggplot(all_contrib, aes(x=species, y=reorder(variable, percent_contribution), fill=percent_contribution)) +
  geom_tile(color="white") + scale_fill_viridis_c() +
  labs(title="Variable Contributions by Species", x="Species", y="Variable") +
  theme_minimal() + theme(axis.text.x=element_text(angle=45,hjust=1))



```

```{r}
fig_dir <- "…/Figures"
dir.create(fig_dir, showWarnings=FALSE)
ggsave(file.path(fig_dir,"variable_contributions.png"), p, width=10, height=8, dpi=600)
ggsave(file.path(fig_dir,"variable_contributions.pdf"), p, width=10, height=8)
print(p)
```

## FREE UP MEMORY

# Function to list objects by memory size

```{r}
# Function to list objects by memory size
big_objects <- function(n = 10) {
  objs <- ls(envir = .GlobalEnv)
  obj_sizes <- sapply(objs, function(x) object.size(get(x)), simplify = TRUE)
  obj_sizes <- sort(obj_sizes, decreasing = TRUE)
  df <- data.frame(
    Object = names(obj_sizes),
    Size_MB = round(as.numeric(obj_sizes) / 1024^2, 2)
  )
  head(df, n)
}

big_objects(15)  # top 15 biggest objects



```

```{r}
# === FUTURE VARIABLES ===
```
