sampled_vals_fut <- terra::as.data.frame(
  terra::spatSample(stack_current, size = 10000, na.rm = TRUE, method = "random")
)

# Step 2: Filter only numeric columns
numeric_cols_fut <- sampled_vals[sapply(sampled_vals_fut, is.numeric)]

# Step 3: Check correlation on numeric data only
cor_matrix_future <- cor(numeric_cols, use = "complete.obs", method = "pearson")

high_cor_pairs_future <- which(abs(cor_matrix_future) > 0.8 & abs(cor_matrix_future) < 1, arr.ind = TRUE)

cor_pairs_future <- unique(t(apply(high_cor_pairs_future, 1, sort)))
colnames(cor_pairs_future) <- c("Var1", "Var2")
correlated_df_future <- as.data.frame(cor_pairs_future)
correlated_df_future$correlation <- mapply(function(i, j) cor_matrix_future[i, j], correlated_df_future$Var1, correlated_df_future$Var2)

cat("== Highly correlated variable pairs (FUTURE, r > 0.8) ==\n")
print(correlated_df_future)

# Optional: VIF for future

#####UPDATED CODE######\

```{r}
# -----------------------------
# STEP 1: Load libraries
# -----------------------------

# Data manipulation
library(dplyr)         # For data manipulation and wrangling (e.g., mutate, filter, summarize)

# Spatial data handling
library(sf)            # For handling spatial vector data (simple features)
library(terra)         # For raster and spatial data manipulation (more modern than raster)
library(raster)        # For raster data processing and compatibility with legacy functions

# Species distribution modeling
library(dismo)         # For species distribution models, especially MaxEnt and background point generation
library(mgcv)          # For fitting Generalized Additive Models (GAMs)

# Machine learning models
library(randomForest)  # For building Random Forest models for classification
library(caret)         # For model training, cross-validation, and evaluation

# Model performance metrics
library(pROC)          # For calculating AUC (Area Under Curve) and ROC curves
library(ROCR)          # Alternative package for ROC curves and performance visualization

# Ensemble and SDM tools
library(enmSdm)        # For ensemble modeling and SDM utilities (used optionally here for extension)

# Visualization
library(ggplot2)       # For custom plotting of results, especially model evaluation
library(RColorBrewer)  # For high-quality color palettes (used in prediction map visualization)

```

\

\
1.LOAD LIBRARY

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(terra)
library(sf)
library(caret)
library(mgcv)
library(randomForest)
library(maxnet)
library(pROC)
library(PRROC)
library(broom)
library(ggplot2)
library(tmap)
library(gridExtra)

```

2.LOAD DATA

```{r}
fia_csv1 <- fia_csv %>%
  mutate(
    Acacia_mearnsii_presence = ifelse(Species_1_Acacia_mearnsii_Perc > 0, 1, 0),
    Acacia_melanoxylon_presence = ifelse(Species_2_Acacia_melanoxylon_Perc > 0, 1, 0),
    Alnus_acuminata_presence = ifelse(Species_3_Alnus_acuminata_Perc > 0, 1, 0)
  )

acacia_mearnsii_points <- fia_sf %>% filter(Acacia_mearnsii_presence %in% c(0, 1))
acacia_melanoxylon_points <- fia_sf %>% filter(Acacia_melanoxylon_presence %in% c(0, 1))
alnus_acuminata_points <- fia_sf %>% filter(Alnus_acuminata_presence %in% c(0, 1))

```

3.Create Forest Mask from Landcover Raster

```{r}
# Load landcover raster
landcover <- rast("path_to_landcover.tif")

# Optional: check class codes
unique_vals <- unique(values(landcover))
print(unique_vals)

# Define forest landcover classes (adjust these as per your legend)
forest_classes <- c(50, 60, 70)

# Initialize all as 0
forest_mask <- classify(landcover, 
                        rcl = matrix(c(-Inf, Inf, 0), ncol = 3, byrow = TRUE),
                        right = NA)

# Mark forest areas as 1
forest_mask[landcover %in% forest_classes] <- 1
forest_mask[landcover %notin% forest_classes] <- 0

# Optional: Save mask
# writeRaster(forest_mask, "forest_mask.tif", overwrite = TRUE)

```

\
4.Define Predictor Stacks (Current and Future)

```{r}
# Current (1981–2010)
predictors_current <- c(elevation, slope, landcover, soil, bio1, bio4, bio5, bio8, bio12, bio14, bio15, bio18)
names(predictors_current) <- c("elevation", "slope", "landcover", "soil", "bio1", "bio4", "bio5", "bio8", "bio12", "bio14", "bio15", "bio18")

# Future (SSP2-4.5)
predictors_future <- c(elevation, slope, landcover, soil, bio1_45, bio4_45, bio5_45, bio8_45, bio12_45, bio14_45, bio15_45, bio18_45)
names(predictors_future) <- c("elevation", "slope", "landcover", "soil", "bio1", "bio4", "bio5", "bio8", "bio12", "bio14", "bio15", "bio18")

```

\
5.SDM Model Fitting & Evaluation Function

This function runs CV for GAM, RF, MAXENT, returns model metrics,
ensemble predictions, and variable importance plot

```{r}
run_sdm_species <- function(species_name, presence_column, points_sf, predictors_stack) {
  message("Processing species: ", species_name)

  env_data <- terra::extract(predictors_stack, vect(points_sf))
  model_data <- data.frame(env_data)
  model_data$presence <- points_sf[[presence_column]]
  model_data <- model_data %>% drop_na()

  n_pres <- sum(model_data$presence == 1)
  if (n_pres < 30) {
    folds <- nrow(model_data)
    method <- "LOOCV"
    indices <- 1:nrow(model_data)
  } else {
    folds <- 3
    method <- "3-fold"
    indices <- createFolds(model_data$presence, k = folds)
  }

  aucs <- c()
  model_preds <- list()
  model_metrics <- list()

  for (model_type in c("GAM", "RF", "MAXENT")) {
    preds <- rep(NA, nrow(model_data))

    if (method != "LOOCV") set.seed(123)

    for (fold in 1:folds) {
      if (method == "LOOCV") {
        train <- model_data[-fold, ]
        test <- model_data[fold, , drop = FALSE]
        test_idx <- fold
      } else {
        test_idx <- indices[[fold]]
        train <- model_data[-test_idx, ]
        test <- model_data[test_idx, ]
      }

      if (model_type == "GAM") {
        mod <- gam(presence ~ ., data = train, family = binomial())
        pred <- predict(mod, test, type = "response")
      } else if (model_type == "RF") {
        set.seed(123)
        mod <- randomForest(as.factor(presence) ~ ., data = train)
        pred <- predict(mod, test, type = "prob")[, 2]
      } else if (model_type == "MAXENT") {
        mod <- maxnet(p = train$presence,
                      data = train[, -which(names(train) == "presence")],
                      f = maxnet.formula(train$presence, train))
        pred <- predict(mod, test, type = "cloglog")
      }

      preds[test_idx] <- pred
    }

    roc_obj <- roc(model_data$presence, preds)
    pr_obj <- pr.curve(scores.class0 = preds[model_data$presence == 1],
                       scores.class1 = preds[model_data$presence == 0])
    tss <- max(sensitivity(roc_obj, "specificity") + specificity(roc_obj, "specificity") - 1)

    model_metrics[[model_type]] <- list(AUC = auc(roc_obj), AUC_PR = pr_obj$auc.integral, TSS = tss)
    aucs <- c(aucs, auc(roc_obj))
    model_preds[[model_type]] <- preds
  }

  # Weighted ensemble prediction
  weights <- aucs / sum(aucs)
  ensemble_pred <- Reduce("+", Map("*", weights, model_preds))

  # Variable importance from GAM coefficients
  gam_model <- gam(presence ~ ., data = model_data, family = binomial())
  var_imp <- tidy(gam_model) %>% filter(term != "(Intercept)")
  plot_imp <- ggplot(var_imp, aes(x = reorder(term, estimate), y = estimate)) +
    geom_point() +
    geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = estimate - std.error, ymax = estimate + std.error), width = 0.2) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray") +
    coord_flip() +
    labs(title = paste("Variable Contribution -", species_name),
         x = "Environmental Variables", y = "Effect Estimate")

  return(list(metrics = model_metrics, ensemble = ensemble_pred, var_plot = plot_imp))
}

```

6.Run SDMs for Each Species (Current Climate) #Repeat this for future
climatic data

```{r}
results_mearnsii <- run_sdm_species("Acacia mearnsii", "Acacia_mearnsii_presence", acacia_mearnsii_points, predictors_current)
results_melanoxylon <- run_sdm_species("Acacia melanoxylon", "Acacia_melanoxylon_presence", acacia_melanoxylon_points, predictors_current)
results_alnus <- run_sdm_species("Alnus acuminata", "Alnus_acuminata_presence", alnus_acuminata_points, predictors_current)

```

7.  Project Model to Full Raster (Current Climate)

```{r}
# Project final calibrated models over full study area
project_full_map <- function(species_name, presence_column, points_sf, predictors_stack) {
  message(paste0("Projecting full map for ", species_name))

  # Extract training data
  env_data <- terra::extract(predictors_stack, vect(points_sf))
  model_data <- data.frame(env_data)
  model_data$presence <- points_sf[[presence_column]]
  model_data <- model_data %>% drop_na()

  raster_pred_list <- list()

  for (model_type in c("GAM", "RF", "MAXENT")) {
    message(paste0("  Model: ", model_type))

    if (model_type == "GAM") {
      final_model <- gam(presence ~ ., data = model_data, family = binomial())
      pred_map <- terra::predict(predictors_stack, final_model, type = "response")

    } else if (model_type == "RF") {
      set.seed(123)
      final_model <- randomForest(as.factor(presence) ~ ., data = model_data)
      pred_map <- terra::predict(predictors_stack, final_model, type = "prob", index = 2)

    } else if (model_type == "MAXENT") {
      final_model <- maxnet(p = model_data$presence,
                            data = model_data[, -which(names(model_data) == "presence")],
                            f = maxnet.formula(model_data$presence, model_data))
      pred_map <- terra::predict(predictors_stack, final_model, type = "cloglog")
    }

    names(pred_map) <- paste0(species_name, "_", model_type, "_risk")
    raster_pred_list[[model_type]] <- pred_map
  }

  return(raster_pred_list)
}

```

7.Project Models Current\

```{r}

proj_mearnsii_current <- project_full_map("Acacia_mearnsii", "Acacia_mearnsii_presence", acacia_mearnsii_points, predictors_current)
proj_melanoxylon_current <- project_full_map("Acacia_melanoxylon", "Acacia_melanoxylon_presence", acacia_melanoxylon_points, predictors_current)
proj_alnus_current <- project_full_map("Alnus_acuminata", "Alnus_acuminata_presence", alnus_acuminata_points, predictors_current)


```

8\. Project SDMs to Future Climate

```{r}
proj_mearnsii_future <- project_full_map("Acacia_mearnsii", "Acacia_mearnsii_presence", acacia_mearnsii_points, predictors_future)
proj_melanoxylon_future <- project_full_map("Acacia_melanoxylon", "Acacia_melanoxylon_presence", acacia_melanoxylon_points, predictors_future)
proj_alnus_future <- project_full_map("Alnus_acuminata", "Alnus_acuminata_presence", alnus_acuminata_points, predictors_future)


```

9\. Create Ensemble Suitability Maps**.** If you want to create an
ensemble map (weighted average of models), you can do this for each
species and climate scenario.

```{r}
ensemble_map <- function(raster_list, weights) {
  stopifnot(length(raster_list) == length(weights))
  weighted_rasters <- Map(function(r, w) r * w, raster_list, weights)
  Reduce(`+`, weighted_rasters)
}

# Example weights (based on AUC or your preference)
weights <- c(GAM = 0.3, RF = 0.4, MAXENT = 0.3)



ensemble_mearnsii_current <- ensemble_map(proj_mearnsii_current, weights)
ensemble_melanoxylon_current <- ensemble_map(proj_melanoxylon_current, weights)
ensemble_alnus_current <- ensemble_map(proj_alnus_current, weights)

ensemble_mearnsii_future <- ensemble_map(proj_mearnsii_future, weights)
ensemble_melanoxylon_future <- ensemble_map(proj_melanoxylon_future, weights)
ensemble_alnus_future <- ensemble_map(proj_alnus_future, weights)
```

### 

10\. Evaluate Risk Using Forest Mask

```{r}

# PLACE: After `ensemble_map()` creation, BEFORE plotting or analysis
evaluate_risk <- function(ensemble_raster, presence_data, forest_mask = NULL) {
  extracted_vals <- terra::extract(ensemble_raster, vect(presence_data))
  obs <- presence_data$presence
  preds <- extracted_vals[[1]]
  
  valid <- !is.na(preds)
  obs <- obs[valid]
  preds <- preds[valid]

  roc_obj <- pROC::roc(obs, preds)
  threshold <- pROC::coords(roc_obj, "best", ret = "threshold", best.method = "youden")
  binary_map <- ensemble_raster >= threshold

  if (!is.null(forest_mask)) {
    masked <- mask(binary_map, forest_mask, maskvalues = 0)
    percent_at_risk <- 100 * (global(masked, fun = "sum", na.rm = TRUE)[[1]] / 
                              global(forest_mask, fun = "sum", na.rm = TRUE)[[1]])
  } else {
    percent_at_risk <- NA
  }

  return(list(binary_map = binary_map, threshold = threshold, percent_at_risk = percent_at_risk))
}

```

## **11. Call Risk Evaluation**

```{r}
# Forest_mask` is defined elsewhere as a binary raster (1 = forest)
risk_mearnsii <- evaluate_risk(ensemble_mearnsii_current, acacia_mearnsii_points, forest_mask)
risk_melanoxylon <- evaluate_risk(ensemble_melanoxylon_current, acacia_melanoxylon_points, forest_mask)
risk_alnus <- evaluate_risk(ensemble_alnus_current, alnus_acuminata_points, forest_mask)


```

12.Mean Risk and Hotspot Map

```{r}
# Mean ensemble suitability across species (stack and average)
mean_risk <- mean(c(ensemble_mearnsii_current, 
                    ensemble_melanoxylon_current, 
                    ensemble_alnus_current), na.rm = TRUE)

# Create binary hotspot map based on threshold (e.g. ≥ 0.6)
hotspot_map <- mean_risk >= 0.6

```

13.Plot Current vs. Future Suitability

```{r}
plot_current_vs_future <- function(current_raster, future_raster, species_name) {
  p1 <- tm_shape(current_raster) + 
    tm_raster(title = "Current Suitability", palette = "YlOrRd", style = "cont", n = 10) +
    tm_layout(main.title = paste(species_name, "- Current"), legend.outside = TRUE)

  p2 <- tm_shape(future_raster) + 
    tm_raster(title = "Future Suitability (SSP2-4.5)", palette = "YlGnBu", style = "cont", n = 10) +
    tm_layout(main.title = paste(species_name, "- Future"), legend.outside = TRUE)

  tmap_arrange(p1, p2, ncol = 2)
}

```

```{r}
plot_current_vs_future(ensemble_mearnsii_current, ensemble_mearnsii_future, "Acacia mearnsii")
plot_current_vs_future(ensemble_melanoxylon_current, ensemble_melanoxylon_future, "Acacia melanoxylon")
plot_current_vs_future(ensemble_alnus_current, ensemble_alnus_future, "Alnus acuminata")

```

Plot Side-by-Side Maps

```{r}
library(tmap)
# Plot side-by-side current vs future maps
plot_current_vs_future <- function(r_current, r_future, species_name) {
  p1 <- tm_shape(r_current) + 
    tm_raster(title = "Current", palette = "YlOrRd", style = "cont", n = 10) +
    tm_layout(main.title = paste(species_name, "- Current"))

  p2 <- tm_shape(r_future) + 
    tm_raster(title = "Future (SSP2-4.5)", palette = "YlGnBu", style = "cont", n = 10) +
    tm_layout(main.title = paste(species_name, "- Future"))

  tmap_arrange(p1, p2, ncol = 2)
}

```

```{r}
# Plot maps for each species
plot_current_vs_future(ensemble_mearnsii_current, ensemble_mearnsii_future, "Acacia mearnsii")
plot_current_vs_future(ensemble_melanoxylon_current, ensemble_melanoxylon_future, "Acacia melanoxylon")
plot_current_vs_future(ensemble_alnus_current, ensemble_alnus_future, "Alnus acuminata")

```

OR plot top and down\

```{r}
library(tmap)
library(gridExtra)

```

```{r}
# Create each map with consistent color scale
m1 <- tm_shape(ensemble_mearnsii_current) + 
  tm_raster(title = "", palette = "YlOrRd", style = "cont", n = 10) +
  tm_layout(main.title = "Acacia mearnsii - Current", main.title.position = "center")

m2 <- tm_shape(ensemble_melanoxylon_current) + 
  tm_raster(title = "", palette = "YlOrRd", style = "cont", n = 10) +
  tm_layout(main.title = "Acacia melanoxylon - Current", main.title.position = "center")

m3 <- tm_shape(ensemble_alnus_current) + 
  tm_raster(title = "", palette = "YlOrRd", style = "cont", n = 10) +
  tm_layout(main.title = "Alnus acuminata - Current", main.title.position = "center")

m4 <- tm_shape(ensemble_mearnsii_future) + 
  tm_raster(title = "", palette = "YlGnBu", style = "cont", n = 10) +
  tm_layout(main.title = "Acacia mearnsii - Future", main.title.position = "center")

m5 <- tm_shape(ensemble_melanoxylon_future) + 
  tm_raster(title = "", palette = "YlGnBu", style = "cont", n = 10) +
  tm_layout(main.title = "Acacia melanoxylon - Future", main.title.position = "center")

m6 <- tm_shape(ensemble_alnus_future) + 
  tm_raster(title = "", palette = "YlGnBu", style = "cont", n = 10) +
  tm_layout(main.title = "Alnus acuminata - Future", main.title.position = "center")

```

```{r}
tmap_arrange(
  m1, m2, m3,  # Top row: Current
  m4, m5, m6,  # Bottom row: Future
  ncol = 3, nrow = 2
)

```
